Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: A PostLoadFcn?callback within the library having commented blocks causes its instance in the model to uncomment
Description: A?PostLoadFcn? callback within the library having commented blocks causes its instance in the model to uncomment.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant block feeding reusable subsystem may lead to incorrect code generation
Description: For a model that meets the following conditions, the code generator might generate incorrect code for the reusable subsystem output function: The model contains at least two instances of the same reusable subsystem. Each reusable subsystem has a Constant block as an input. Each Constant block has a different Constant value  parameter. For the Sample time  parameter, the Constant blocks have the default value of inf . In the Model Configuration parameters dialog box, on the Optimization>Signals and Parameters  pane, the Default parameter behavior  parameter is set to Inlined  and the Inline invariant signals  parameter is selected.

Summary: Variant subsystem fail to build when using bitsets to store state configuration/boolean data
Description: Variant subsystem with generate pre-processor conditionals will fail to build if model configuration have below settings. 1. In model configuration: BooleanAsBitFields set to true? ?? for models with Stateflow, the following are true in addition to the condition above.? 2. In model configuration 'StateAsBitSets' and 'DataBitSets' set to true. The generated code from variant subsystem will have misplaced substructure definition like below:  ? /* Block states (auto storage) for system '<Root>' */typedef struct {? struct {??? uint_T is_active_c1:1;??? uint_T is_c1_repr_16b:1;#if Variant? } bitsForTID0;? DW_min__spd;#define DW_16B__T_VARIANT_EXISTS#endif???????????????????????????????? /* Variant */} DW_reproduced_16b__T; ? ?  ? ? ? ?
Workaround: Turn off the below model configuration settings which manages bitfields in generated code.1. When the model has stateflow charts?as?variant choice blocks:? 'Use bitsets for storing state configuration'??? 'Use bitsets for storing Boolean data '??? No need to?turn off these parameters in the absence of Stateflow charts.?2.?When the model has only Simulink blocks:?'Pack Boolean data into bitfields'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code may be incorrect for a model that contains chained Merge blocks that span reusable subsystem boundaries
Description: When Merge blocks cross reusable subsystem1  boundaries, the generated code may fail to pass the output signal to the function call resulting in incorrect code. The code generator may generate this code for a model that meets the following conditions. Note that the subsystem names in parentheses refer to the names in the example model attached to this EBR. The model contains a reusable subsystem (ss_bottom ) that contains multiple blocks that connect to a Mux block. The outport of the Mux block connects to the outport of ss_bottom . A reusable subsystem (ss_middle ) contains ss_bottom  and a Merge block. The outport of the Merge block connects to the outport of ss_middle . The above Merge block connects to another Merge block in a parent subsystem. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization>Signals and Parameters  pane, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Individual arguments .  The attached example model and images and the code snippet below show a case in which the generated code is uncompilable because g1388596_example_B  is only accessible in the local scope. For some models, g1388596_example_B  may be defined globally. For those models, the generated code compiles but could possiby give wrong answers. void g1388596_example_SS_middle(real32_T rtu_AThreshStat) {  if (rtu_AThreshStat == 1.0F) {g1388596_example_SS_bottom(&g1388596_example_B->Merge[0],&g1388596_example_B->Merge[1]);   }  }void g1388596_example_step(RT_MODEL_g1388596_example *const g1388596_example_M,  real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN1, real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN3, real_T  g1388596_example_Y_RootOutPort[2]){...  if (!(g1388596_example_U_DIN1 == 1.0F)) {g1388596_example_SS_middle(g1388596_example_U_DIN3);   }...  }  The generated code does not contain the output of SS_middle  in its function call. The definition of SS_middle  instead refers to a variable (g1388596_example_B->Merge ) that is only accessible inside the top level model. The following code snippet shows the correct generated code: void g1388596_example_SS_middle(real32_T rtu_AThreshStat, real_T rty_MidOutPort[2])  {  if (rtu_AThreshStat == 1.0F) { g1388596_example_SS_bottom(&rty_MidOutPort[0], &rty_MidOutPort[1]);   }  }  void g1388596_example_step(RT_MODEL_g1388596_example *const g1388596_example_M,  real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN1, real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN3, real_T  g1388596_example_Y_RootOutPort[2]){  ...  if (!(g1388596_example_U_DIN1 == 1.0F)) {g1388596_example_SS_middle(g1388596_example_U_DIN3,  g1388596_example_B->Merge);   }  ...}  The correct code contains the output of ss_middle  (that is, rty_MidOutPort ) in its function call. 1  For a subsystem, the code format may be reusable if on the Block Parameters dialog box, the Code Generation>Function packaging  parameter is set to Reusable function  or Auto .
Workaround: The following are some possible workarounds:For multiple Merge blocks in a chain, only the last one in the chain should connect to a reusable subsystem outport.Insert a nonvirtual block in between the Merge blocks. Note that this workaround causes extra memory consumption since previously all the inputs to the Merge block share the same variable.Do not connect multiple blocks to a Mux block and then connect this Mux block directly to a chain of Merge blocks.Change subsystem packaging to inline or nonreusable.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible when an Assignment block and a Unit Delay block are in the same For iterator subsystem
Description: The code generator may generate incorrect code for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a For Iterator subsystem.  The For Iterator subsystem contains an Assignment block and a Unit Delay block. In the Assignment block parameters dialog box, the Index Option  parameter is set to Index vector (port)  or Starting index (port).  The?Initialize output (Y)  parameter is set to Specify size for each dimension in table.  In the Unit?Delay block parameters dialog box, the Initial condition  parameter has initial values.  In the generated code, the Unit Delay block output may?be incorrect. For example, the Unit Delay block output may be partially updated at each simulation step.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated C/C++ code for a recursive MATLAB function inside Stateflow Chart may be incorrect if the function contains a large variable
Description: The code generator may generate incorrect C/C++ code for a Stateflow Chart that meets the following conditions: The Stateflow Chart contains a MATLAB function and a graphical function. The MATLAB function calls the graphical function which calls the same MATLAB function. The MATLAB function contains a local variable that is large enough to exceed the value of the TLC variable MaxStackVariableSize . For a Windows target, the default value of this variable is 4096 bytes.  ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multiple subsystems incorrectly share the same state variable for Unit Delay blocks
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? Incorrect code generation might occur when all of the following conditions are true: Optimize global data access ?configuration parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results ?or Minimize global data access  Reuse local block outputs  configuration parameter is enabled. Multiple subsystems in the model have their?Function packaging  parameter set to Auto  or Reusable.  Unit Delay blocks' input or output are connected to those subsystems' output signal.  Errors might be thrown during the build process.
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized size vector in generated code
Description: For some cases of multiple complete assignments to an array, the generated code contains an unitialized size vector. Your C compiler might warn that a variable is uninitialized. This issue can occur when two different parts of the code assign all elements of an array to different values. For example: function o = myfcn(x,n)assert(n==5);y = zeros(2,n);for k = 1:size(y,1)    y(k,:) = x(k,:);endo = int32(y); This issue can occur for any code generation product.
Workaround: Download the attached p-file and install it in a folder on the MATLAB path.Rewrite the code to call the p-file before the assignment in the part of the code that performs the second assignment. Here is the modified code for the previous example:function o=fcn(x,n)assert(n==5);y=zeros(2,n);for k = 1:size(y,1)   if nonConstTrueFixUpToR2016b()     y(k,:)=x(k,:);   endendo=int32(y);

Summary: Error message occurs on Windows stating that the build has failed
Description: The build fails on Windows with an error message similar to the following: NMAKE : fatal error U1073: don't know how to make 'C:\Program'  Stop.  This can occur if MATLAB is installed into a path with spaces, which is the case for the default install location of: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R20YYx . The Simulink Coder build process normally accomodates the limitation of makefile utilitites with respect to spaces in paths by populating the makefile variable ALT_MATLAB_ROOT  with the Windows short filename (SFN), usually C:\PROGRA~1 . However, it is becoming more common for Windows systems to be configured with the support of SFN disabled when using the New Technology File System (NTFS) or for Windows to be configured to use the newer Resilient File System (ReFS), which does not support SFN. Note: The Windows short filename (SFN) is sometimes called the 8.3 filename.
Workaround: Workarounds include any of these:Re-install MATLAB into a path without any spaces.Manually map a network drive to the "MATLAB" folder of the installation that has spaces in the path. For example if you installed MATLAB at c:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2016aRun the Windows command: net use * "\\<computer_name>\C$\Program Files\MATLAB".Start MATLAB using the newly mapped drive letter: Y:\R2016a\bin\matlab.exe instead of using the MATLAB icon or Start menu.If using NTFS, configure NTFS to enable short filename (SFN) support: Go to Windows Start menu and search for regedit.exe and run it.Navigate to the following location: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem\NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreationChange the value of NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreation to 2 or 0. (Note: 2 is the default setting for Windows NTFS.)For more information on this registry setting, see: https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/cc778996(WS.10).aspx

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for a Variant Subsystem that is nested in control flow may be missing a required curly brace
Description: The generated code for a Variant Subsystem that is nested in control flow may be missing a required curly brace if the following are true: The generated code for the Variant Subsystem is in the false branch of a control flow construct. On the Code Generation>Comments  pane, the Simulink block/Stateflow object comments  parameter is not selected.  Without the curly brace, the generated code does not compile. For example, in the following generated code snippet, the code for the Variant Subsystem is in the else  statement, and the else  statement is false. As a result, at the beginning of the else  statement, the generated code is missing a required curly brace. if (x)  {...code...}  else  # if VARIANT  code for variant1  #else  code for variant2  #endif}
Workaround: To workaround this issue, you can try selecting the Simulink block/Stateflow object comments parameter.

Summary: Abs block might have incorrect coverage when the input and output of the Abs block have different data types
Description: The Abs block might report incorrect coverage when the input and output of the Abs block have different data types. For example: If the input to the Abs block is negative, it should satisfy the "U < 0" objective.??The coverage highlighting and report indicates that the "U < 0" objective was not satisfied even though it clearly was.

Summary: Uncompilable code may be generated for custom S-function that has expression folding enabled
Description: The code generator may generate uncompilable code for a top-level model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a function-call subsystem. The function-call subsystem contains a custom S-function block which uses the function LibBlockSetIsExpressionCompliant()  to enable expression folding.  The generated code may be uncompilable due to uninitialized variable(s). ?
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:Extract the files from the .zip file.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Running a model from a working directory with an existing slprj folder can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB can crash if there is an existing slprj  folder in your working directory when you run your model containing Stateflow blocks or MATLAB Function blocks. The crash occurs when there is an issue writing to the slprj  folder (for example, you do not have write access permission).
Workaround: To reduce the possibility of crashing, run the attached p-file when you start your MATLAB session. However, if you do not have write permission to the slprj folder, you will still get an error message when you try to run the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for MATLAB function block or Stateflow Chart?containing variable-sized arrays may be incorrect
Description: For a model that meets either of the following conditions, the generated code may contain incorrectly-sized output array:  The model contains a MATLAB function block with a variable size output. The model contains a Stateflow Chart with a variable size local or chart output.  The MATLAB function or Stateflow Chart changes the size of the variable by doing one of the following: A null assignment operation like var(rows,:) = [];  A user-defined function with reusable in/out arguments with?the same name.  The incorrect generated code has a temporary size array that is a copy of the variable. This copy is passed to a function call along with the original data array. ?The function updates the temporary size array. ?The following is a sample of the incorrect generated code: tmp_sizes[0] = matrix_size[0];tmp_sizes[1] = matrix_size[1];functionCall(matrix_data, tmp_sizes);
Workaround: Change the name of either the input argument or output argument of the MATLAB function, so that the names are no longer the same and avoid using the null assignment on the chart variables or?MATLAB function output.

Summary: Code generated for variant subsystem does not compile
Description: When a variant subsystem is configured to generate preprocessor conditionals, and the variant subsystem contains a triggered subsystem, you may generate code that fails to compile. The error message is similar to the following: model.h:162:8: error: duplicate member _rt_unused

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model blocks with multiple function-call input ports driven by Function Call Split blocks using Simulink Accelerator execute the wrong function calls during simulation
Description: Model blocks with multiple function-call input ports that are driven by Function Call Split blocks when used in a top model simulated using Simulink Accelerator either fails to generate code or executes the wrong function call port during simulation, leading to incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: Change the simulation mode of the top model to something other than Accelerator.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generating code for a model with floating point to fixed point conversions may result in incorrect generated code
Description: During the code generation process, Simulink may ignore the model setting Use floating-point multiplication to handle net slope corrections  when performing internal calculations. This parameter can be found in the Configuration Parameters > Optimization  pane.  If the bug is triggered, values in the generated code will differ from simulation values. This difference is the result of using floating-point division versus floating-point multiplication of a reciprocal. The following conditions must be true to trigger this bug: The Use floating-point multiplication to handle net slope corrections  parameter is set to On.  The model contains conversions from constant floating-point values to fixed-point types that have non-power of two slopes.
Workaround: Set the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Use floating-point multiplication to handle net slope corrections parameter to Off.Alternatively, at the command-line enter:set_param(Model_name, 'UseFloatMulNetSlope', 'Off')This workaround will impact all conversions from floating point to fixed point.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible rounding error or overflow due to incorrect translation of division and multiplication operations
Description: If the design of a model results in division or multiplication operations involving fixed-point or integer types, incorrect overflow or rounding error might occur when: In the case of division, the denominator is reduced to a constant value of?-1 In the case of multiplication, either operand?is reduced to a constant value of -1  The incorrect overflow only occurs if the other operand is a signed type and it takes on the minimum negative value for its type during runtime.  For the rounding error to occur, the type of the other operand must be fixed-point and have a fractional part.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for MATLAB Function block that uses negative array offsets
Description: Embedded Coder may generate incorrect code for a MATLAB Function block that uses a negative offset from a loop variable to assign values to matrix elements. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Division operations specified with saturation on integer overflow and nontrivial scaling incorrectly replaced with division implementation that wraps on integer overflow
Description: If any of the following conditions are true, a code replacement library (CRL) can cause the code generator to incorrectly replace a division operation for saturation on integer overflow and nontrivial scaling with a call to an implementation function that wraps on integer overflow: An input or output type of the division operation has a nonzero bias. Net slope adjustment factor (net-saf ) is not equal to 1, where net-saf  is calculated as numerator_saf /(output_saf *denominator_saf ) .

Summary: Comments in Truth Tables using non-ASCII characters are corrupted when saved to MDL format
Description: If the description field of a Truth Table contains non-ASCII characters, saving the model in MDL format (not SLX), closing it, and then reopening it displays garbled characters instead of the original ones.
Workaround: The corruption on the comments happens when saving the model to MDL format.  MDL models created in previous releases are not affected by loading them. Saving those models to SLX format avoids the problem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect analysis results reported for multi-rate and multi-tasking models
Description: For multi-rate and multi-tasking models, Simulink Design Verifier may return incorrect analysis results under either of the following model configuration settings: Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Sample Time >?Multitask rate transition ?is set to 'None' . Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Sample Time >?Multitask rate transition ?is set to?'Warning' and Configuration Parameters > Solver >?Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is unchecked.  Under such configurations, the generated test case or counter example may not satisfy the objective during simulation or the reported design range may be incorrect.?

Summary: ?S-function parameterized by reference to struct parameter may cause model build to fail
Description: A model may fail to build if it ?contains an S-Function with parameter usage as follows: The S-function is parameterized by a reference to a struct  field. The struct  field has an array type.  ?

Summary: Embedded Coder generates C code that references an undefined variable when a model contains multiple instances of subsystems connected to Unit Delay blocks
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? Embedded Coder may generate C Code that is not compilable because it references an undefined variable when a model meets these conditions: The model contains a subsystem that has a Unit Delay block which directly connects to the subsystem's output port. There are multiple instances of the subsystem In the subsystem block parameters dialog box, Function packaging  is set to Reusable Function . In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Structure reference . In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Optimize global data access  parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results? or?Minimize global data access.
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.

Summary: Parameter tuning in the presence of other errors can fail silently
Description: In certain cases, tuning parameters during simulation can fail to change the simulation result if either  of these conditions is met: A function referenced by a block is modified in a way which causes a parameter evaluation error. (R2016a only) A parameter on a block in a referenced model is modified while there are many other parameter evaluation errors. This only applies to Fast Restart simulations.  In either case, a subsequent update diagram reports success, but may not tune the parameter values of these blocks.
Workaround: To address this problem, use one of these approaches: After you have resolved the errors, call an additional set_param on the affected block.After you have resolved the errors, open the parameter dialog box of the affected block and press OK.Add a reference to a base workspace variable from the affected block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Nested if statements with identical conditions containing function calls with side effects may be incorrectly combined into one if statement in the generated code
Description: Simulink Coder may generate code that incorrectly combines nested if  statements that meet the following conditions: The if  statements contain identical conditions. The conditions contain calls to functions that have side effects such that each call can produce a different result.  For example, the if  statements in the code below may be incorrectly merged into one if  statement: Code before merging if  statements: if (rollDice()==7) {  printf("found seven once");  if (rollDice()==7) {    printf("found seven twice");  }}  Code after merging if  statements: if (rollDice()==7) { printf("found seven once"); printf("found seven twice");}  The above if  statements should not be combined in the generated code because rollDice()  may produce a different result with each function call. For example, for the attached model, the code generator assumes that the value of a  is constant and shrinks multiple if  statements with calls to this function to a single function call. As a result, there is no code for braches A2 , A3 , A4 , and A5 .

On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Certain operations might be evaluated unexpectedly when used in a logical expression
Description: If you use certain operations like cast or array indexing as part of a logical expression, the operations might get incorrectly evaluated outside of the logical expression in the generated code. This issue affects Stateflow transitions, MATLAB functions in Stateflow, MATLAB Function blocks, and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink. For example,?a transition condition of: [u < arrayLen && int32(myArray[u]) < 5]  might generate the following code: tmp = myArray[u];?if (tmp < MAX_INT) {?? ?if (tmp > MIN_INT) {?? ? ? tmp_0 = tmp;?? ?} else {?? ? ? tmp_0 = MIN_INT;?? ?}?} else {??? ?tmp_0 = MAX_INT;?}?if (u < arrayLen && tmp_0 < 5) {?...??  Notice that the array is indexed in the first line, before checking whether or not the index is valid.? ?
Workaround: Break up the array indexing or cast operation into three transition segments. The first transition?contains the index check. The second consists of a call to an empty Simulink function. The third transition consists of the second part of the original logical expression. For example, the first transition could have:[u < arrayLen]?followed by a second transition with:?{emptyfcn();}followed by a third transition with:?[int32(myArray[u]) < 5]?

Description: The eig function might generate incorrect eigenvectors when all of the following conditions are true: A column or row of the matrix has at most one nonzero entry. The nonzero entry in that column or row, if any, is on the main diagonal. The corresponding row or column has a nonzero entry that is off the main diagonal.  This problem does not occur for triangular matrices. This problem can cause incorrect results for x^y  when x  is a scalar and y  is a matrix, or when x  is a matrix and y  is a scalar.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation when branched signals are present in Variant choice blocks feeding into an outport
Description: When one or more variant choices inside a Variant Subsystem block contain branched signals, the code generated from the model is incorrect. The incorrect code generation occurs when there is no contiguous source of memory for the output ports of the Variant Subsystem block.
Workaround: Inside variant choices that contain branched signals, insert signal conversion blocks that feed the root output ports.

Summary: ForEach subsystem code might incorrectly reuse variable from its containing model's code
Description: ForEach subsystem code?might incorrectly reuse a variable from its containing model's code, leading to wrong answers, if all of the following conditions are true: Optimize global data access  configuration parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results  or Minimize global data access . Enable local block outputs  configuration parameter is enabled. Reuse local block outputs  configuration parameter is enabled. In the ForEach subsystem block parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  tab, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Auto  or Inline .  The ForEach subsystem variable that gets increased or decreased with every iteration of the for  loop might get?used as an array index after the for ?loop processes.

Summary: Embedded Coder may fail to build a model when a Unit Delay block directly connects to a reusable subsystem output
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? A model containing a subsystem may fail to build if the following conditions exist: On the Subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, Function packaging  is set to Reusable Function . The subsystem contains a Unit Delay block which directly connects to a subsystem output port. On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Individual Arguments  rather than the default Structure reference  On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Combine signal/state structures  parameter is selected. On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize global data access ?parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results? or?Minimize global data access   When the build failure occurs, the diagnostic output contains an errror message similar to the following: ### Build procedure for model: '...' aborted due to an error.Error: File: .....Indices may not be negative
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Saturation port of Discrete-Time Integrator block can give incorrect results
Description: The saturation port of the Discrete-Time Integrator block outputs 3 possible values ('1.0', '0' and '-1.0') indicating the saturation status of the block's output port. It allows only double , single  or int8  data types. This port can give incorrect results if the block gets output data types from back-propagation that gives an int8  data type to the saturation port but a double  data type to the output port. This happens when:  The block's OutputDataTypeStr  parameter is set to Inherit via back propagation  Input port data type is double  The signal coming out from the output port does not specify any data type The signal coming out from the saturation port has the int8  data type, due to back propagation.
Workaround: If both input and output ports have double or single data type, make sure that the saturation port has the same data type as the output port. If an int8 signal from the saturation port is required, connect the saturation port to a Data Type Conversion block to convert all possible values ('1.0', '0' and '-1.0') from double or single data type to int8.

Summary: Unnecessary generated code for a model that contains an Assignment block inside a For Iterator subsystem
Description: Simulink Coder may generate unnecessary code in the model _initialize  function for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains an Assignment block inside a For Iterator subsystem On the Assignment block parameters dialog box, the Initialize output (Y)  parameter is set to Initialize using input port <Y0> .  The model_initialize  function may also contain an uninitialized local variable that later gets written to.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized local variable is used for the output buffer of Unit Delay block
Description: In the generated code, an uninitialized local variable might be used for the output buffer of a Unit Delay block when all of the following conditions are true: Optimize global data access ?configuration parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results?or  Minimize global data access.   Reuse local block outputs? configuration parameter is enabled. The Unit Delay block output is connected only to Stateflow Charts or State Transition Tables where the Chart/Table's Function Packaging  is set to either Auto  or Inline .
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727. That report describes a different but related issue, and the same workaround applies.?

Summary: Unable to load deployable archives that require MATLAB Runtime version 8.5.1 (R2015aSP1)
Description: Deployable archives (.ctf files) that require MATLAB Runtime version 8.5.1 (R2015aSP1) fail to load when deployed on a MATLAB Production Server of a different version.  Note that this bug applies if you're using MATLAB Production Server R2015a (non-SP1) as well. The error message states that there is an unknown MATLAB Runtime version 0.0.
Workaround: Use MATLAB Production Server R2015aSP1 to run deployable archives that require MATLAB Runtime version 8.5.1 (R2015aSP1).

Summary: Embedded Coder may fail to build a model that reuses local block outputs and contains a Unit Delay block
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? In rare cases, a model containing a subsystem and Unit Delay block may fail to build if the following conditions exist: On the Subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, Function packaging  is set to Reusable Function . On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization >Signals and Parameters  pane, Reuse local block outputs  is enabled.. On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Structure reference . On?the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize global data access ?parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results? or?Minimize global data access   When the build failure occurs, the diagnostic viewer contains one or both of the these error messages: Error: File: ...\syslib.tlc Line: 2725 Column: 55The + operator only works on numeric arguments  or Error: File: ...\syslib.tlc Line: 2725 Column: 56 The specified index ... was out of the range 0 - ...
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Call to a persistent function that reads a persistent variable without writing it, and has its own code to error if the persistent variable is uninitialized may produce wrong answers
Description: Incorrect code can result from a function with a persistent variable, designed so that some call sites read the persistent and other call sites write the persistent.? The function must also have explicit checks to report an error if the persistent is not initialized in the calls that read it (for example using assert or error). Here is an example that illustrates this behavior:? function out = exfcn(1);out = fcn();disp('see this?');end function y = fcn(u)persistent p if nargin == 1    p = u;end assert(~isempty(p)); % This assertion causes the wrong behavior.y = p;end To see the wrong behavior: 1. Generate the MEX function. ??codegen ex  2. Run the MEX. ?ex_mex
Workaround: ?Remove the?test ?that the persistent variable is not empty from the code.? ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Switch statement?with a?character?switch expression and noncharacter case expressions, or vice versa, may result in a wrong answer in generated code
Description: The generated code can differ from MATLAB for a switch statement with: A?character switch expression and noncharacter case expressions A noncharacter switch expression and character case expressions  ?In these cases, the code generator excludes the case expressions for the nonmatching type, regardless of the value. For example, consider this code: x = 'a';switch x    case double('a')        disp(1);    otherwise        disp(2);end ?In?MATLAB, this code displays 1, but the generated code displays?2. This issue occurs in MATLAB Coder, a MATLAB Function block, MATLAB in Stateflow, and a MATLAB System block..?
Workaround: Make sure that the switch expression and case expressions are either all character or?all noncharacter.??Convert the switch and?case expressions to the larger type, usually the noncharacter type.For example:x = 'a';switch x    case double('a')        y = 1;    otherwise        y = 2;endshould be rewritten as:x = 'a';switch double(x)    case double('a')        y = 1;    otherwise        y = 2;end

Summary: Coverage analysis fails when a referenced model in accelerated mode references other models
Description: If all of the following conditions are true, Simulink displays an error when you attempt to run coverage analysis on a model containing a multi-level hierarchy of referenced models. The Simulation Mode  parameter of a Model block is set to Accelerator The referenced model itself references one or more other models The referenced model is not already opened or loaded when performing coverage analysis  The following error message is displayed:  Input must be a structure.Caused by:    A fatal error occurred when accessing the coverage tool: cvi.TopModelCov.modelStart
Workaround: When working with a multi-level hierarchy of model references, ensure that any model reference which is set to Accelerator simulation mode, and also contains references to other models, is loaded or opened before beginning coverage analysis.

Summary: Copying and pasting commented blocks causes segmentation violation
Description: A segmentation violation can occur when you copy and paste parts of your model that contain commented-out or commented-through blocks.
Workaround: If parts of your model contain commented-out or commented-through blocks, uncomment those blocks before a copy-paste operation.

Summary: Commands bdroot and gcb return incorrect paths after copying and pasting blocks that have test harnesses.
Description: If you copy a block that has a test harness, and paste it in the model canvas, bdroot  returns a path that incorrectly points to one of the pasted test harnesses instead of returning the name of the current top-level system.  The command gcb  incorrectly returns the handle of a block in the test harness.

Summary: Concatenation operation may cause code generation failure
Description: Simulink Coder may crash during code generation for a model that contains a Matrix Concatenate block that concatenates two or more 2-D signals so as to produce a 3-D output signal.

Summary: Opening a test harness for a pasted block results in an unexpected assertion
Description: If you perform either of the following two sequences of operations, then try to open a test harness for the pasted block, Simulink returns an unexpected assertion, "Invalid Simulink Object Handle." Select a block with test harnesses, then copy, delete, and paste the block in the Simulink editor. Select a block with test harnesses and then copy and paste the block.  Then, delete the original block and paste again.

Summary: Performing cut, undo, and paste operations on a block with test harnesses results in a pasted block without test harnesses.
Description: If you select a block and the block has one or more test harnesses, then you perform the following sequence of operations on the block: Cut Undo Paste   The pasted block does not contain the test harness(es).

Summary: "libGL error: unable to load driver" during MATLAB? startup
Description: During MATLAB startup, you might see output like this in the Linux console: libGL error: dlopen /usr/lib64/dri/nouveau_dri.so failed (/usr/local/MATLAB/R2014b/bin/glnxa64/../../sys/os/glnxa64/libstdc++.so.6: version `GLIBCXX_3.4.18' not found (required by /lib64/libLLVM-3.5-mesa.so))libGL error: unable to load driver: nouveau_dri.solibGL error: driver pointer missinglibGL error: failed to load driver: nouveaulibGL error: dlopen /usr/lib64/dri/swrast_dri.so failed (/usr/local/MATLAB/R2014b/bin/glnxa64/../../sys/os/glnxa64/libstdc++.so.6: version `GLIBCXX_3.4.18' not found (required by /lib64/libLLVM-3.5-mesa.so))libGL error: unable to load driver: swrast_dri.solibGL error: failed to load driver: swrast This output indicates that MATLAB is unable to load the graphics driver and will use software OpenGL? instead.
Workaround: These errors indicate that MATLAB is using software OpenGL? instead of the graphics driver. Thus, you can safely ignore the error messages.

Summary: Range analysis using Fixed-Point Designer may produce no results for models with large arrays
Description: Running range analysis using Fixed-Point Designer may produce no results for models with arrays of more than 200 constant integer or fixed-point elements. The analysis finishes without showing any errors, but the range results are empty. Examples where such arrays can originate include block parameters and MATLAB variables.
Workaround: To get results from the analysis, set the Data type override parameter of the model to double.

Summary: Viewmark not saved with model if spaces in subsystem path
Description: Simulink reports an error when you save a model with a viewmark under certain conditions. When this error appears, Simulink saves the model but does not save the viewmark. This error occurs when: A subsystem in the model has a space in its hierarchy path name. You create a viewmark of the subsystem. You copy the viewmark to the viewmarks gallery to save with the model.   ?
Workaround: You can work around this problem using one of the two following workarounds.Remove the space in the subsystem hierarchy path.Unzip the attached file and put viewmarker.p in (matlabroot)/toolbox/shared/dastudio/+DAStudio/@Viewmarker, where (matlabroot) is the root folder of your MATLAB installation. Execute >> rehash toolboxcache from the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: Crash when running model slicer on models with unconnected lines
Description: Running Model Slicer on a model with unconnected lines might result in a MATLAB crash with symptoms similar to the following  [  0] 0x00007faac4e2e44b ggb_commented(SLBlock const*) [  1] 0x00007faac51b4dec isCommentedThrough [  2] 0x00007faac51b4bef visitSegment [  3] 0x00007faac51b4fa8 walkThroughBlock
Workaround: Remove all unconnected lines from the model

Description: MATLAB might crash when running model slicer on a model that has a referenced model whose input or output is a nested bus. The generated stack trace begins with either of the following: [  0] 0x00007fede61db55b addLeaves [  1] 0x00007fede61dc6cc SubBus::leaves() const[  2] 0x00007fede61dcaae SubBus::isSubsetOf[  3] 0x00007fede61c416c updateReach [  4] 0x00007fede61c6235 visitBusCreator  or [  0] 0x00007ff77af7759d visitBusSelectionVar[  1] 0x00007ff77af452ad SLDataflowGraphWrapper::forwardReachable[  2] 0x00007ff77af50943 mcos::factory::impl::MethodInvokerImpl[  3] 0x00007ff7d0a3d117 OpaqueMethodCallMetaData::callCMethod[  4] 0x00007ff7d0a3eeb6 OpaqueMethodCallMetaData::callMethodOrConstructor

The crash occurs when attempting to do any of the following: Simulate the model in Rapid Accelerator mode Simulate another model that references this model Generate code for the model  The generated stack trace contains the error message: failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\src\stateflow\export\include\stateflow\vector.hpp at line 340: Tried to access beyond Vector length

This issue can also appear in the HDL test bench, when 'UseFileIOInTestbench' is set to 'off'.

Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. To see the contents of the .zip file:                                       Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a terminal window :unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>                                          Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a terminal window:                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: dbquit or Ctrl+C does not stop simulation when paused at a breakpoint
Description: When you are paused at a breakpoint in a Stateflow chart or MATLAB Function block, you cannot stop the simulation using dbquit  or Ctrl+C .
Workaround: Use the Stop Simulation button instead.

Summary: Generated code for Direct Lookup Table block may contain C math floor() function instead of C implicit cast
Description: For an index expression that must be positive, the Direct Lookup Table block requires a float to integer type conversion. The generated code may contain a C math floor()  function to handle this type conversion even though a C implicit cast produces the same result as floor()  and is less expensive.

Summary: Global variable input definition section missing from MATLAB? Coder? app without Fixed-Point Designer?
Description: If the Fixed-Point Designer? software is not installed, the global variable input definition section  is missing from the Define Input Types step of the MATLAB?  Coder? app.

Summary: Rename All does not replace variable name in model argument list
Description: When you use a model as a referenced model, you can declare variables in the referenced model workspace as model arguments. When you use Rename All  in the Model Explorer to rename one of these variables, the operation does not replace the name of the variable in the model argument list.
Workaround: Manually replace the variable name in the model argument list.For example, in the Model Hierarchy pane of the Model Explorer, select the target model workspace. In the Dialog pane, replace the variable name in the Model arguments (for referencing this model) field.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Binary-point multiplication operations incorrectly replaced with MulShRight AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library (CRL) entry
Description: The code generator can incorrectly replace a binary-point multiplication operation with a MulShRight  AUTOSAR 4.0 CRL entry if all of the following conditions are true:Code generator is configured to use the AUTOSAR 4.0 CRL. Ideal result of the multiplication operation is signed. Multiplication operation rounds to zero.  The AUTOSAR 4.0 specification describes MulShRight  as arithmetic shift right, which is equivalent to rounding to floor.  Given the preceding conditions, code replacement of a multiplication operation that rounds to zero with MulShRight  is incorrect.
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1435708 to download a patch that addresses this and other bugs.

Summary: Stateflow charts using atomic subcharts with local data of Bus type may encounter unexpected errors
Description: Stateflow throws an error when a chart contains at least two levels of atomic subcharts, with a data store memory on the lowest level mapped to a local data object on the middle level, and the local data is of type Bus .  Error using tlc_c (line 179)     The index of the bus element is specified as '0'. This value is not valid for the region of memory 'blkpath/ SFunction ' associated with the block 'DataName' The error message may refer to a different data object, but it is the data store that is causing the problem.
Workaround: Change the scope of mid-level local data object to Output. You can connect the output port to a terminator block on the Simulink canvas if necessary.

Summary: MATLAB throws an error related to "jarego.jayatana" during startup
Description: Upon starting MATLAB in an Ubuntu environment with Unity global menu enabled, the following exception is thrown in the Linux command line: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.awt.IllegalComponentStateException: component must be showing on the screen to determine its location at java.awt.Component.getLocationOnScreen_NoTreeLock(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Component.getLocationOnScreen(Unknown Source) atcom.jarego.jayatana.swing.SwingGlobalMenuWindow.componentResized(SwingGlobalMenuWindow.java:549) at java.awt.AWTEventMulticaster.componentResized(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Component.processComponentEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Component.processEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Container.processEvent(Unknown Source) at java.awt.Window.processEvent(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.mwswing.MJFrame.processEvent(MJFrame.java:868) at java.awt.Component.dispatchEventImpl(Unknown Source) In addition, accessing menu items does not work as expected.  This is happens because this new Ubuntu Uniti global menu feature interferes with MATLAB's Java features. Note that this Unity global menu feature is enabled by default starting in Ubuntu 15.04.
Workaround: Prior to starting MATLAB, check the JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS environment variable. If this variable contains the following string, remove it:-javaagent:/usr/share/java/jayatanaag.jarNote that you will need to do this step when starting MATLAB from a new Linux shell as this change only applies to the current shell.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when state names are longer than 25 characters
Description: In Stateflow charts using MATLAB as the Action Language, using state names longer than 25 characters might cause MATLAB to crash when simulating the model or generating code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code for AUTOSAR model with inport configured for ExplicitReceive data access
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for an AUTOSAR model if all of the following conditions are true: A root-level Inport block is connected to a Switch block in a subsystem. A Data Type Conversion block is present on the signal path between the root-level Inport block and the Switch block. In the AUTOSAR configuration of the model, the AUTOSAR data access mode of the root-level inport is set to ExplicitReceive .  Under these conditions, the generated C code might incorrectly omit a call to the access function for the root-level inport.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. Summary: Unsaved test harnesses are discarded if you attempt to save a model to a read-only directory
Description: Attempting to save a model to a directory for which you do not have write permission results in the following error: Error using save_system (line 38)Error writing file "C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015b\foo.slx":  copy failed: fl:filesystem:AccessDenied Permission denied  If the model you attempted to save contains test harnesses that were not previously saved, the harnesses are lost.

Summary: "Invalid intersection between states ..." error when simulating/building a model with Test Sequence blocks.
Description: Occasionally, a model with Test Sequence blocks fails to simulate or build with the following error message in the diagnostic viewer: Invalid intersection between states ...  When this happens, one or more Test Sequence blocks may be corrupted, and can only be recovered by manually recreating these blocks. This typically happens for Test Sequence blocks that are edited heavily by adding and removing steps repeatedly.

Summary: Subsystem variants with Function packaging set to Inline might cause uncompilable code
Description: When all of the following conditions are met, the code generator might incorrectly place the subsystem function return statement: A model contains a subsystem that encloses a variant subsystem. There is one outport port from the variant subsystem and the subsystem that encloses the variant subsystem. For the subsystem variants, the Function packaging  is set to Inline . For the subsystem that encloses the variant subsystem, the Function packaging  is set to Nonreusable function  and the Function interface  is set to Allow arguments .  For example, in the attached model (VariantSubsystemReturn.slx), the generated code is uncompilable because of a misplaced return statement in the subsystem function.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:If you want to leverage the subsystem function return value, set the Function packaging for the variant subsystems to Nonreusable function and the Function interface to Allow arguments.If you do not want to leverage the substem function return value, set the Function packaging for the variant subsystems to Nonreusable function and the Function interface to void_void.

Summary: Generating C++ class interface code for a Model block with variants can cause TLC error
Description: C++ class code generation for a model throws an error if both  of the following conditions exist: The model contains a Model block with variants. The referenced model is also configured to generate a C++ class.  Under these conditions, the software generates a TLC error similar to the following: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefutil.tlc Line: 1114 Column: 7Ambiguous reference to ModelRefInstanceVar - must use array index to refer to one of multiple scopes

Summary: Exporting a Test Harness discards harness model parameters
Description: Exporting a test harness discards the model parameters for the harness model and replaces them with default parameters. Therefore, saved parameters are lost when a test harness is exported to a separate model.  This behavior occurs for both ways of exporting a test harness:Selecting File > Export Model To > Independent Model  Using the sltest.harness.export  function

Summary: Error when simulating or generating code after switching between variants with Stateflow charts
Description: Models using linked variant subsystems in which one variant contains Stateflow charts and the other does not can produce the following error during simulation or code generation after the active variant is switched to one in which there are no Stateflow charts within the variant subsystem.  Unable to open block template file lib_MODEL_NAME .tlc from GENERATE or GENERATE_TYPE.
Workaround: After switching the active variant, delete the slprj folder and the generated mex file (if any) associated with the model; this will regenerate code for the entire model. You may need to execute the clear mex command to unload the mex file from memory before deleting it.

Summary: Slow analysis performance for models with many Data Store Memory blocks
Description: If your model contains many Data Store Memory blocks, this can negatively impact Simulink Design Verifier analysis performance.

Summary: Stateflow transitions are invisible while being created until the mouse is dragged over a state/junction
Description: Normally, transitions are visible while being created, even if the endpoint of the transition is over an empty space. Due to this bug, the transition only becomes visible once the endpoint is state or junction.

Summary: Moving a box inside of a locked model crashes Simulink
Description: Moving a box (area) inside of a locked model crashes Simulink. A model is locked if it has a lock icon in the bottom left of the canvas.
Workaround: Unlock the library or disable the link to the library to move the box.

Description: If a test case uses a verification test harness created for an entire model, the test case fails unexpectedly and locks the model.

Summary: MATLAB crash caused by deleting a block with a broken library link that contains a test harness, then saving the model
Description: MATLAB crashes if you delete a block that meets both of the following criteria, and then save the model. The block has a test harness.  The block is an instance of a library link, and the link is broken.

Summary: Resizing a uibuttongroup object makes child components disappear in GUIDE
Description: Resizing a uibuttongroup  object might cause its child components, such as radio buttons or toggle buttons, to disappear. This behavior occurs while viewing the UI layout in GUIDE when both of the following are true: The Visible  property of the uibuttongroup  object is set to 'off'  The Units  property for each child component is set to 'characters'   MATAB?, however, correctly displays the contents of the uibuttongroup  at run time.
Workaround: Change the Visible property of the uibuttongroup object to 'on' before resizing the UI layout.

Summary: Unable to open MATLAB? Coder? app project in earlier release after opening in R2015a?
Description: In R2015a, if you open a  project from an earlier release, the MATLAB?  Coder? app updates the project file to the R2015a format. Because of this format change, you cannot open the project in the earlier release. ?
Workaround: In R2015a, before you use the MATLAB Coder app to open a project from an earlier release, create a backup of the project file. Use the backup project file in the earlier release.

Description: If you override signal logging when a large number of signals are configured to log, simulation can be quite slow. The simulation can get stuck in the 'initialization' phase for an extended period of time. Overriding signals involves one  of these actions: Use the Signal Logging Selector to override signals. From the command line, create a ModelLoggingInfo  object, using the Simulink.SimulationData.ModelLoggingInfo.createFromModel  API, modify some of the settings on signals in the Signals  property, and then set the model to use this override: set_param('model', 'DataLoggingOverride', mli)

Summary: Model block SIL or PIL simulation can generate code for unrelated referenced models
Description: Simulating a Model block in the software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) mode, with the Model block Code interface  parameter set to Model reference , can trigger code generation for unrelated referenced components. The unnecessary code generation occurs when the block path of the unrelated referenced component contains the name of a referenced model that is in SIL or PIL mode. Consider a top model, Logic_Harness , which contains Model blocks A  and B. In addition: A  references the Logic  model. B  references the UDP_SendReceive  model.  Simulating Logic_Harness/A  in SIL or PIL mode triggers an unnecessary build of UDP_SendReceive . This build occurs because Logic_Harness  contains the substring Logic . This issue can lead to: Longer build times for SIL and PIL simulations. Errors if the unrelated referenced components do not fully support code generation.
Workaround: Rename models. Make sure that the names of referenced models simulating in SIL or PIL mode are not substrings of the block path of any other Model block. For the example above, you can change the name of the Logic_Harness model to Harness.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when?passing an empty character array to the?'%s'?format specifier of?fprintf
Description: If you pass a fixed-size empty character array to the fprintf  format specifier '%s'  in?your MATLAB code, the generated code behaves incorrectly. For example,?the?MATLAB code could contain this expression: fprintf('%s','')  The incorrect behavior?can be printing out something like '(null)' , printing out garbled data, or a runtime crash. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow??

Description: When model's Shared code placement  configuration parameter is set to shared utilities , Simulink Coder will incorrectly use the Code  code-generation template (".cgt") file for the variable declarations instead of the Header  ".cgt" file.

Summary: bdroot is incorrect after sltest.harness.load
Description: After loading a test harness using the function sltest.harness.load , bdroot  points to the main model when the function should point to the loaded test harness.
Workaround: Programmatically select a block in the Test Harness model using set_param(blkHandle, 'Selected', 'on') to ensure bdroot is set correctly.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: n-D Lookup Table blocks with different integer rounding modes in the same model can generate incorrect code
Description: n-D Lookup Table blocks in a model can generate incorrect code if all  of these conditions are met. The data types for the various parameters of the n-D Lookup Table block are in the Data Types  tab in the block parameters dialog box. The model has multiple almost identical n-D Lookup Table blocks with different settings for Integer rounding mode . The data type of the Fraction  parameter is a floating-point type. The data type of the Intermediate results  parameter has a higher precision than the data type of the Table data  parameter. The data type of the Output  parameter is an integer or fixed-point type. The data type of the Output  and Intermediate results  parameters are different. The data type of the Output  parameter has a lower precision than the datatype of the Intermediate results  parameter. Index search method  is set to Linear search  or Binary search .  In this case, all the blocks call only one function, which uses the integer rounding mode of the first n-D Lookup Table block.
Workaround: In the n-D Lookup Table block, set the data type of the Output parameter to be the same as the Intermediate results data type. Use a Data Type Conversion block to cast the output of the n-D Lookup Table block to the required type with the desired integer rounding mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Integer overflow may be incorrectly missing from generated code
Description: For a model design that results in generated code in which an integer is cast to a smaller integer type, so that overflow occurs, the generated code may be missing the overflow, resulting in wrong answers.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Literal character escape sequence might be incorrect?in generated code
Description: In rare circumstances, the generated code might contain an incorrect literal character escape sequence. For example the generated code might contain an unterminated character such as the following code: myModel_fcnCall('  instead of the correct escape sequence '\x00' . The correct code is as follows: myModel_fcnCall('x00');?  This issue might occur for a modeling pattern consisting of all of the following elements?: A MATLAB Function block? The use of character constants corresponding to a literal escape sequence?such as ?'char(0) ' in the MATLAB Function block? The character constant is passed as an argument to a function call

Summary: From File block leaks memory when loading underspecified bus signal data
Description: When you load a bus signal using a From File block, you can specify a subset of leaf signals. For the rest of the leaf signals (underspecified signals), Simulink uses corresponding ground values. Each underspecified signal leaks a small amount of memory. A large number of underspecified signals can cause a substantial leak. The leaked memory is not released until the MATLAB process is finished.
Workaround: Provide data for each leaf signal when loading a bus signal using a From File block.

Summary: Undoing or redoing port modification can cause Simulink to crash or give incorrect simulation results
Description: Simulink can crash or give incorrect simulation output if you undo or redo a parameter change in a block which involves modifying a parameter port or signal output ports. This happens because of the incorrect state of ports of that block after the undo/redo operation. Blocks affected by this bug include, but are not limited to, Delay Enabled Delay Resettable Delay Variable Integer Delay Interpolation Using Prelookup Entity Departure Counter Entity Departure Function-Call Generator Entity Sink Event-Based Entity Generator FIFO Queue Get Attribute Infinite Server Input Switch Priority Queue LIFO Queue N-Server Output Switch Path Combiner Resource Acquire ResourcePool Resource Release Set Attribute Single Server Signal-Based Function-Call Generator Time-Based Function-Call Generator Time-Based Entity Generator

Summary: SIL with BullseyeCoverage does not show coverage results for _sharedutils code
Description: To collect code coverage data for generated code, you can run a software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation that uses the BullseyeCoverage tool. You can view code coverage results using the BullseyeCoverage browser or the code generation report. However, the code generation report does not display code coverage annotations for generated code in the _sharedutils  folder. You can still view code coverage results for code in the _sharedutils  folder using the BullseyeCoverage browser. However, the browser displays results for only the most recent simulation. The browser does not display cumulative results.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier does not support Lookup Tables with Nearest Interpolation Method
Description: On models containing Lookup Tables with Nearest  Interpolation method, Design Verifier analysis does not provide information about the unsupported block and produces the following error message:  Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: < model > is incompatible with Simulink Design Verifier.
Workaround: Change the Interpolation Method of affected Lookup Table blocks.

Summary: Slow operations in the MATLAB? Coder? app
Description: The MATLAB Coder app checks entry-point functions for code generation readiness. For some large entry-point functions, or functions with many calls, these checks can take a long time. If the checks take a long time, certain app operations can be slower than expected, or appear to be unresponsive.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Passing a colon as an argument to a function handle generates code instead of throwing an error
Description: In MATLAB, passing colon operator to a function handle is not allowed:? >> f = @(x) x+1;  >> f(:)  Input arguments to function include colon operator. To input the colon character, use ':' instead.   However, MATLAB Coder will generate code for this, instead of throwing an error.

Summary: Inaccessible Model Advisor checks and functionality for seperately-installed MathWorks products
Description: If you install a MathWorks toolbox or blockset separately after you have installed Simulink, then when Model Advisor tries to perform checks, it incorrectly assumes that the installed toolbox or blockset is not available. In that case:  Model Advisor checks provided with the seperately-installed product are not available. Model Advisor does not perform checks that depend on the separately-installed product.
Workaround: In your MATLAB preferences folder, delete the maproductinfo.mat cache file. To find the the preferences folder, at the MATLAB command line, enter prefdir.Restart MATLAB and open the Model Advisor.

Summary: Subsystem analysis fails for subsystems that are contained within masked subsystems
Description: Extracting a subsystem for subsystem analysis fails when one of the parents of the analyzed subsystem is a masked subsystem. The analyzed subsystem is reported as being incompatible, and the following error message is displayed: Content for extraction must not be commented out or part of an inactive variant

Summary: ModelSim crashes with large HDL Coder test benches
Description: When HDL Coder generates an HDL test bench with a sufficiently large number of test vectors, the generated HDL can cause ModelSim, a third-party HDL simulator, to crash on Linux. The number of test vectors that cause this fault is variable and depends on your environment.
Workaround: Consider generating a test bench that places the test vector data in external data files, by selecting the Configuration Parameters > HDL Code Generation Test Bench > Use file I/O to read/write test bench data checkbox. Alternatively, at the command line, enter: hdlset_param(model_name,'UseFileIOInTestbench','on')If this workaround is not possible or desired, you can also work around the problem by adjusting the Linux shell environment from which you launch MATLAB, by increasing available stack space. The command to use is specific to the command shell you use.For bash/sh:$ ulimit -s <value_in_kb> For tcsh/csh:% limit stack <value_in_kb> The value for the stack size that will allow a simulation to succeed can must be determined by experimentation. Doubling the current value is a reasonable value to start with.

Summary: ROS code generation fails on Linux or Mac platforms with "Unable to find success string" error
Description: When you attempt to generate a standalone ROS node from a Simulink model, it can fail with the following error, where 'robotExROSFeedbackControlExample' is a Simulink example model: ### Build procedure for model: 'robotExROSFeedbackControlExample' aborted due to an error.Error: ### Unable to find build success string: "### Successfully generated all binary outputs." in build log. The problem occurs when: You attempt to generate code in a MATLAB session running on Linux or Mac, and Your default shell is configured to bash , or related shells such as sh , dash , or ksh
Workaround: A temporary workaround is to specify tcsh or csh as the default shell before starting MATLAB, assuming tcsh or csh is already installed on your machine.export SHELL=`which tcsh`matlab A permanent workaround is to install the attached patch.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:    Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>     Extract the files from the .zip file.   Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                          Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: XCP channel in a deployed application generates error
Description: If you try to create and run an XCP channel in a deployed application, the following error message appears, even if the code runs without error within MATLAB on the same machine-hardware combination: Error using xcp.Channel/connect (line 187)The channel was unable to create an XCP protocol object connected to the specified transport layer device. Please ensure that your device is connected and available for use. ?
Workaround: View Bug Report ID 1205811 to download a patch to address this issue.

Summary: Unable to preview webcam in a deployed application
Description: If you try to preview webcam in a deployed application, the following error message is displayed, even if the code runs without error within MATLAB on the same machine-hardware combination: Error using asyncioimpl.ChannelFailure to load the converter plug-in.Error in asyncio.Channel (line 97)Error in internal.IntervalTimer (line 53)Error in matlab.webcam.internal.PreviewController/preview (line 99)Error in webcam/preview (line 267)Error in webcamCompiledApp (line 3)asyncio:Channel:couldNotLoadConverter
Workaround: You can visit Bug Report ID 1205811 to download a patch that addresses this issue.

Summary: Unable to preview ipcam in a deployed application
Description: If you try to preview ipcam in a deployed application, the following error message is displayed, even if the code runs without error within MATLAB on the same machine-hardware combination: Error using ipcamCompiledApp (line 4)Failure to load the converter plug-in.asyncio:Channel:couldNotLoadConverter
Workaround: You can visit Bug Report ID 1205811 to download a patch that addresses this issue.

Summary: Unable to use the TimerFcn and TimerPeriod properties of audioplayer and audiorecorder in deployed mode
Description: Compile the MATLAB? function shown below:function testFuncar = audiorecorder;ar.TimerFcn = @(~, ~) disp('Timer Fired');ar.TimerPeriod = 0.5;recordblocking(ar, 5);  into a standalone executable using MATLAB Compiler? usingmcc -m testFunc  Attempting to run this executable against MATLAB Compiler Runtime? (MCR) results in the following error:Error using asyncioimpl.ChannelFailure to load the converter plug-in.Error in asyncio.Channel (line 97)Error in internal.IntervalTimer (line 53)Error in audiorecorder/initializeTimer (line 445)Error in audiorecorder/initialize (line 248)Error in audiorecorder (line 193)Error in testFunc (line 3)asyncio:Channel:couldNotLoadConverter
Workaround: You can visit Bug Report ID 1205811 to download a patch that addresses this issue.

Summary: Unable to preview matroxcam and gigecam in a deployed application
Description: If you try to preview matroxcam and gigecam in a deployed application, the following error message is displayed, even if the code runs without error within MATLAB on the same machine-hardware combination: Error using camCompiledApp(line 2)Failure to load the converter plug-in.asyncio:Channel:couldNotLoadConverter
Workaround: You can visit Bug Report ID 1205811 to download a patch that addresses this issue.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier displays error on models containing For Each Subsystem blocks with multiple mask parameters
Description: On models containing For Each Subsystem blocks with more than one mask parameter, Design Verifier displays the following error message:  Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated C code for Simulink function call array arguments using AUTOSAR schema version 3.x
Description: Embedded Coder generates incorrect C code for a model configured for the AUTOSAR target if both of the following conditions exist: In the model, a Simulink Function Caller block calls a Simulink function with an array argument. AUTOSAR schema version 3.x is selected for the model.  Under these conditions, the model build generates function call argument code that is appropriate for schema version 4.x, but not appropriate for 3.x. For example: real_T (*tmp)[2];...Rte_Call_cport_Fcn(rtb_callFcn, *tmp); For AUTOSAR schema version 3.x, array arguments are passed as pointer to array type rather than pointer to base type. The function call should not dereference the pointer. For example: real_T (*tmp)[2]; ... Rte_Call_cport_Fcn(rtb_callFcn, tmp); Depending on compiler warning and error settings, compilation might generate an error, or might implicitly cast from one pointer type to another and not trigger an error.

Summary: Dialog boxes of bus blocks inserted using quick insert can show empty signal hierarchy?
Description: If you use the quick insert functionality to create a Bus Selector, Bus Assignment, or Bus Creator block, the block parameters dialog box can show an empty signal hierarchy, even though the block is connected to a bus signal. ?
Workaround: Close and reopen the dialog box of the affected block. Then the signal hierarchy of the incoming bus signal is displayed.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code?generator can incorrectly replace math functions when I/O arguments are matrix types
Description: When?configured to use?a?code replacement library, the code?generator calls incorrect implementation functions if all  of these conditions are true: You configure the code generator to use a code replacement library?that includes?entries for math functions that accept matrix inputs?or?outputs. The?code generator produces multiple calls to the same math function. The different function call instances have different input or output argument matrix type dimensions but the same base type.  Under these conditions, the code generator replaces different instances of the function call with the same implementation function specified in an entry that matches one of the instances. These replacements can result in incorrect answers or crashes when you run the generated code.

To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General. Summary: Parameters dialog box for Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks with masking freezes
Description: When you open the block parameters dialog box for a Data Store Read or Write block, you get a DataStoreElements  text box in the Element Selection/Assignment  tab if both  of these conditions exist: The Data Store Read or Data Store Write block is masked or is in a masked subsystem. The DataStoreName parameter is promoted.  You cannot use the block parameters dialog box until you enter text in the DataStoreElements  text box and click Apply.
