Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code does not contain code for Switch block input that does not route to output
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions: A non-tunable constant value feeds into the Control input signal  parameter of a Switch block. The Threshold  parameter of the Switch block contains a non-tunable value.  Due to the above conditions, at all time steps, the same switchsignal connects to the output. As a result, the code generator might omit the code forthe block feeding into the data input port of the Switch block that does notroute to the output. Omitting this code is incorrect when the signal that feedsinto the data input port of the Switch block that does not route to the outputalso performs an operation such as writing to a Data Store Memory block or a MATLABFunction persistent variable.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Leaf state activity output from an atomic subchart does not get reset when the subchart becomes inactive
Description: If an atomic subchart has leaf state activity output enabled, the output port is not reset to None  when the atomic subchart becomes inactive.
Workaround: Wrap the contents of the atomic subchart in a new state that can serve as a container.Configure the container state to output its leaf state activity.Change the name of the output variable and its enumerated type to match that of the atomic subchart's leaf state activity output.Turn off leaf state activity output on the atomic subchart.Verify that the container state's leaf state activity output is mapped correctly to the parent chart's output.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rounding error may occur in generated code that casts a matrix of complex double to a matrix of complex int
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model design that results in code that casts a matrix containing complex values of floating point type to a matrix containing complex integer types. The?generated code contains incorrect results because the rounding mode is changed from round towards zero or nearest to round to floor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect numerical result or crash due to missing data alignment directives for exported or GetSet custom storage class
Description: Generated model code might produce incorrect numerical results or crash if?all of the following conditions are true: The?compiler for the target processor compiled code that?produces?numeric results that differ from simulation results or crashes when using an instruction that requires specific data alignments?and?the data is not aligned properly. During code generation, a code replacement occurs that requires arguments to be aligned. An argument that requires data alignment is associated with one of the following: A built-in custom storage class with DataScope  set to Exported . For example, the argument references a Simulink data object for which the Storage class  (CustomStorageClass ) attribute is set to ExportToFile . Imported built-in custom storage class GetSet .    Under these conditions, code generation fails to emit data alignment directives for the argument and execution produces incorrect numerical results or crashes. Built-in storage classes ExportedGlobal  and SimulinkGlobal  are not affected by this bug.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during run-time update on an n-D lookup table block in Accelerator mode
Description: MATLAB might crash while performing a run-time update on an n-D lookup table block when all of the following are true: The model is configured to run in Accelerator simulation mode The parameter Index search method  is set to Linear search  or Binary search  The check box Begin index search using previous index result  is selected There is at least one vector input along with one scalar input to the block

Summary: Status of proof objectives reported too conservatively for models containing lookup tables in property proving analysis
Description: For models containing lookup tables and proof objectives dependent on the lookup tables, property proving analysis in some cases may conservatively report the objective status as 'undecided due to stubbing,' i.e. 'proven valid' or 'falsified' even though it is decided.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Type Conversion block with boolean output data type and 'Saturate on integer overflow' selected may produce incorrect generated code
Description: When using Data Type Conversion blocks, incorrect code will be generated if all the following conditions are true: The output data type of the Data Type Conversion block is boolean. The 'Saturate on integer overflow' parameter is selected. The 'Integer rounding mode' for the block is set to either 'Simplest', 'Floor', 'Zero', or 'Ceiling'.
Workaround: To work around this bug, clear the 'Saturate on integer overflow' parameter of the Data Type Conversion block.

Summary: Creating rostf transformation tree results in error messages
Description: When connecting to a ROS network that publishes transformations, the MATLAB transformation tree functionality does not work. When creating a transformation tree by calling rostf , you see multiple red error messages of the form: [ERROR] SubscriberHandshakeHandler - Subscriber handshake failed: Message types don't match.  The AvailableFrames  property of the resulting transformation tree object is always empty (size 0x1), even if transformations are available.

Summary: Simulink Scope unusable after relaunching with blank legends
Description: If you enable Scope legends before running the model, the following warning appears, as expected: Legend information is not currently available. The legend appears when simulation has started.   Then, if you close the scope and save the model, reopening the scope produces the following incorrect error:  M Assert: unexpected nuumber of axes.
Workaround: To launch the Scope for the current model,Close your model.Use clear all to clear the workspace.Relaunch the model.To permanently fix this issue, see the attached file.

Summary: Opening a block diagram test harness throws a warning if the name of the referenced Simulink model in the test harness is changed on disk
Description: When you open a test harness associated with a top-level Simulink model block diagram, if the name of the Simulink model referenced in the test harness has changed on disk, a warning similar to the following is thrown: The model 'mymodel' referenced by 'mymodel_Harness1/mymodel' is not found.  The warning occurs because the model name in the Model block is pointing to the old model name. ?
Workaround: Open the Test Harness.Update the model name in the Model Reference block to point to the updated model name. Re-save the model.

Summary: MSSCCI source control is unavailable in MATLAB R2015a
Description: When Enable Microsoft Source Code Control Interface (MSSCCI)  is selected in the Preferences  > General  > Source Control  pane, source control features are unavailable. For example:In the Current Folder browser context menu, the Source Control  > Register...  menu item is disabled. In the Current Folder browser context menu, selecting Source Control  > Start Source Control System...  results in the following error: No source control system is configured. Select a source control system from preferences.  verctrl('runscc',0)  results in the following error: No source control system is configured. Select a source control system from preferences.  cmopts  returns None .
Workaround: There are two ways to work around the problem: (1) install a patch, or (2) install a Tech Preview of the new MSSCCI integration (MathWorks login required).Method #1: Install a patch       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB.       Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.                     Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Windows Systems*:                                                             Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.                                                                                                Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.       Launch MATLAB.       At the MATLAB command prompt, type:           rehash toolbox           rehash toolboxcache         *Notes for Windows Systems:      You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.    If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you  may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to  WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting  Run as administrator.    From  within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.    Extract  the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.  Method #2: Install a Tech Preview of the new MSSCCI integration (MathWorks login required)    On the Home tab, in the Environment section, click Preferences.    In the preferences dialog box, navigate to the MATLAB > General > Source Control pane.    To use the MathWorks source control integration, which is accessible through the Current Folder browser, select Enable MathWorks source control integration.    Install the tech preview of the new MSSCCI integration by following the steps described here.

Summary: Dead Logic Detection inconsistency when generating tests for models that exhibit run-time errors
Description: Generating tests for models that exhibit run-time errors may satisfy objectives that are identified as dead by Dead Logic Detection.
Workaround: Identify all Integer Overflow, Division-by-Zero, and Out of Bound Array Access errors in the model and fix them before performing Dead Logic Detection or generating tests.

Summary: Error when generating test cases for models with inputs of certain enumeration types
Description: Simulink Design Verifier throws an error when generating test cases for a model with inputs of custom-defined enumeration data types. Cannot find a member of the 'ENUM_CLASS_NAME' enumeration class that corresponds to each element of the given input argument. where ENUM_CLASS_NAME  is the name of the class which defines the enum. This occurs when the enumeration type is defined as a subclass of one of the following built-in data types: int8 , uint8 , int16 , uint16 , or int32 .

Summary: SIL or PIL simulation of AUTOSAR model fails when Simulink function name does not match corresponding client-server operation name
Description: In an AUTOSAR client-server model, an AUTOSAR operation name can differ from the corresponding Simulink function name. However, if the names differ, running a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation on the model fails with an error message that contains the text 'could not find entry for "WriteBlock" in binfo '.

Summary: Test harness created for a model fails to simulate in some cases
Description: Simulink throws an error when you simulate a test harness that meets all of the following conditions: The test harness was created for the model The test harness was modified prior to starting the simulation  The error thrown is similar to the following: Can not update the model reference target of 'myModel' used in 'myModel_Harness1' because it has unsaved changesError while bringing model references of model 'myModel_Harness1' up to date:  see errors reported above for details.
Workaround: Save the model before starting the simulation.

Summary: Clicking the perspectives control button in a model after viewing harness models with Dashboard blocks crashes MATLAB
Description: Executing the following actions will crash MATLAB: Click on the perspectives control on the lower-right corner of a model to view a harness model that contains Dashboard blocks. Click on the perspectives control in the harness model to return to the original model. Click on the perspectives control again in the original model.  ?

Summary: Atomic subcharts with mapped events or mapped parameters can cause a model update error
Description: Simulating or updating a model containing an atomic subchart with mapped events can cause this error message: Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values. Simulating or updating a model containing an atomic subchart with mapped parameters can cause an incorrect error message in the Diagnostic Viewer about data erroneously mapped to chart level data. The error begins: Atomic subchart 'myAtomicSubchart' illegally accesses main chart ....
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Summary: Viewing the contents of a MAT-file in the Current Folder browser details panel may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you view the contents of a MAT-file containing one or more Simulink objects in the details panel of the Current Folder browser without Simulink running, Simulink attempts to start, and possibly crashes MATLAB.
Workaround: If you know that a MAT-file contains Simulink objects, avoid selecting it for preview until Simulink has loaded.

Summary: Zero-crossing data structures generated for a model containing Variant model blocks may be empty
Description: When a model containing variant model blocks is configured to generate preprocessor conditionals, the compiled zero-crossing data structure in the code generated from the model may be empty under certain preprocessor conditions. For example, if a model contained a Variant Model block with two variants, and only the first submodel had a zero-crossing detection, then the zero-crossing data structure for that top model is similar to the following:  typedef struct {#if S1  rtZCE_bot1 Model_1_DWORK1;#endif} PrevZCSigStates_top; If the second variant is the one to be compiled into the executable, so that S1  was false when compiling the code, the structure PrevZCSigStates_top  has no elements.
Workaround: If one of the submodels does not introduce an element into the zero-crossing data structure, add a block to the submodel that results in the creation of a zero-crossing element for that submodel. For example, add a Triggered Subsystem block to a submodel to introduce an element corresponding to the submodel into the top model's zero-crossing data structure.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible?wrong answer?in generated code for Interpolation using Prelookup block related to output data type overflow
Description: Incorrect?code may be generated from the Interpolation using Prelookup block?when these?conditions?are all true: The Saturate on integer overflow  check box is selected  The Interpolation method  is set to?Linear  Table Data Type ,?Intermediate results Data Type ,?and?Output Data Type ?are fixed-point data types Table Data Type  and?Output Data Type  have the same slope adjustment factor and bias Table Data Type ?does not overflow if cast to?Intermediate results Data type  or the?Output Data Type   Intermediate results Data type ?may overflow if cast to?Output Data Type  The?Integer Rounding mode ?is set to either:Ceiling ,?Convergent ,?Round , or?Nearest ?when?the?Table data ?Value is close to the maximum value of the data type Floor ,?Simplest , or?Zero ?when the?Table data ?Value is close to the minimum value of the data type    Computation of the interpolation overflows the?Intermediate results ?Data Type   If these conditions are all present, a wrapping effect may occur instead of a saturation effect in the output of the Interpolation using Prelookup block.
Workaround: Set the??Output data type?parameter of the Interpolation Using Prelookup block to?the same data type as the?Intermediate results data typeAdd and connect a?Data Type Conversion block to the output of the Interpolation Using Prelookup blockSelect the Saturate on integer overflow check box of the Data Type Conversion blockSet the?Output data type?parameter of the?Data Type Conversion block to the desired data type

Summary: Slow packaging process when deploying MATLAB code
Description: When using mcc  or deploytool  to package MATLAB code for deployment, the packaging process is two or three times slower than it was in R2014b for the same code. The MATLAB command matlab.codetools.requiredFilesAndProducts  is also affected by the slow down.
Workaround: Workaround 1Run MATLAB or the mcc or deploytool commands as administrator.Workaround 2Install MATLAB in a location where the user can write to without administrator privileges.Workaround 3Give full read/write permissions to the user for the dependency analysis database files located at:matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\depfun\+matlab\+depfun\+internal\requirements_arch_dfdbmatlabroot\toolbox\matlab\depfun\+matlab\+depfun\+internal\pcm_arch_dbwhere arch is the platform architecture, such as win64, and matlabroot is the MATLAB installation location, such as:C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015aChanging the user permissions may require administrator privileges once.  Any compilation after that should not need it.

Summary: Error when setting a breakpoint in some MATLAB Function blocks that use fixed-point values
Description: When you set a breakpoint on any line of a MATLAB Function block that has both the Allow direct feedthrough  option turned off and uses fixed-point values, Stateflow throws an error when you try to simulate the model: Error using autobuild_kernel>autobuild_local (line 194)Unable to inline 'eML_blk_kernel'. It might be due to recursive calls.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening dialog for bus routing blocks in Simulink
Description: While modifying models using the Simulink editor, users might experience crashes when attempting to open the dialog for bus routing blocks. Bus routing blocks include Bus Creator, Bus Selector, and Bus Assignment.
Workaround: Run the attached MATLAB script BR1220118.p prior to opening the dialog for any offending bus routing blocks. The attached function will only need to be run once per MATLAB session. You can add this script to a startup file (startup.m or matlabrc.m) to run automatically upon MATLAB startup.This script will alter the behavior of the Find button functionality within the dialogs for any bus routing blocks. Use the Highlight Signal to Source right-click option for the signals feeding into the bus routing blocks as a workaround.

Summary: Error when generating tests for subsystems containing enabled or triggered model reference blocks
Description: Generating tests for subsystems that contain Enabled or Triggered model reference blocks will error out. The following error will be thrown: Invalid Simulink object name:

Summary: Unable use rapid accelerator mode to simulate a model that uses the duration operator in a Test Sequence block
Description: If you use the duration  operator in a Test Sequence block, and try to simulate the model in rapid accelerator mode, the following error message is displayed: Code generation and simulation targets registered different sets of persistent variables for the block. External or Rapid Accelerator mode simulation requires code generation and simulation targets to register the same set of persistent variables for this block. This discrepancy is typically caused by MATLAB functions that have different code paths for simulation and code generation targets where these code paths define different sets of persistent variables. Please identify these code paths in the offending block and rewrite the MATLAB code so that the set of persistent variables is the same between simulation and code generation.
Workaround: You may be able to work around this issue by turning off debugging: >> sfc coder_options forceDebugOff 1Note that this turns off animation and debugging for all test sequence blocks and any Stateflow charts in the current MATLAB session.This will also prevent you from setting breakpoints, which could complicate debugging.

Summary: Converting blocks including a Bus Creator block into a subsystem can cause model update or simulation error
Description: If you select a group of blocks in the model and convert the blocks to a subsystem, using either the right-click menu option or the marquee selection action, an error can occur later during model update or simulation if: The set of blocks being converted includes a Bus Creator block. That Bus Creator block is connected directly to one of the subsystem inputs. In other words, there is no intervening block between an input to the converted subsystem and the Block Creator block.  This is the error message that Simulink generates: Selected signal 'SignalName' in the BlockType block 'BlockPath' cannot be found in the input bus signal.
Workaround: Use either of these workarounds:Prior to the creation of the subsystem, disconnect the signals connected to the Bus Creator block. After the conversion, reconnect the signals to the subsystem input ports.After the creation of the subsystem, in the Signal Properties dialog box for each signal reported in the error message, enable the Show propagated signals parameter. To restore the model to its original configuration, you can disable the Show propagated signals parameter for each signal that you just enabled.

Summary: Using a deleted data dictionary variable with fast restart does not error out during simulation
Description: If a data dictionary variable used in a block parameter is deleted and the simulation is using fast restart, the first simulation shows an error about the deleted variable. However, subsequent simulations will not show an error and will use the previously evaluated value.

Summary: Opening Simulink Perspectives can hang MATLAB if the model includes blocks from the Dashboard library
Description: Using the Perspectives button in a model that contains Dashboard blocks can hang MATLAB, with no way of breaking out.

Summary: Unable to deploy the serial command with MATLAB Compiler
Description: If the MATLAB serial  command is deployed, the resulting component may fail with the following error during runtime: Error using serial (line 120)Cannot create: No class com.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.SerialComm can be located on the Java class pathError in testSerial (line 2)MATLAB:serial:serial:cannotCreate
Workaround: Create a file named javaclasspath.txt in your MATLAB preferences directory.  The preferences directory can be found with the prefdir command in MATLAB.In the javaclasspath.txt file, add the following two lines:$matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/instrument.jar$matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/testmeas.jarThe $matlabroot text should be kept as is, do not change it to a specific path.  Restart MATLAB and recompile the component.

Summary: Incorrect linearization Jacobian matrix for Bus Assignment block
Description: For an Bus Assignment block that has a nonvirtual bus input, the linearize  function and the Linear Analysis > Linearize Block  menu item each produce an incorrect Jacobian matrix.
Workaround: Use one of these approaches:Change the nonvirtual input bus to a virtual input bus.Insert a Signal Conversion block before Bus Assignment block to convert the nonvirtual input bus to a virtual bus.

Summary: Code generation error for models with masked reusable subsystems when Inline parameters is cleared
Description: Code generation displays an error when a model meets all of the following conditions: The model configuration parameter Inline parameters  is cleared. The model contains multiple instances of a masked subsystem, which are identical except for the values used to specify the mask parameters. The subsystem has the Function packaging  parameter, on the Code Generation  tab of the subsystem parameters dialog box, set to Auto  or Reusable function . Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model Reference block inside a For Each subsystem may produce incorrect generated code
Description: When a Model Reference block is put inside a For Each subsystem, it is possible that the ForEach  induction variable is used as an array index after the ForEach  loop is processed, resulting in incorrect code. As an example, extract the attached Example.zip and build the model, named top.slx . Note how the variable named forEach_itr_i32  is used as an array index inside the ForEach  loop and then again afterward in expressions for the output ports. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect value for 0th element of static constant boolean array in generated code
Description: Incorrect code is possible for a model design that results in generated code that uses?a boolean constant array or matrix in any of the following contexts: The boolean constant contains both false and true values. This issue does not affect boolean constants in which every element has the same value. The boolean constant is used to compute an element-wise logical OR.  The boolean constant is used to compute an element-wise logical AND.  The boolean constant is used in?the part of an if  statement that evaluates to true or false. The boolean constant is used in the part?of a ternary operator (?: ) that evaluates to true or false. The boolean constant is the control input?to a Switch block.  The attached model issue_example.zip  contains examples of this issue with element-wise logical OR , element-wise logical AND , and a switch input signal. In the generated code this constant has an incorrect value for the 0th element: /* Output and update for atomic system: '<Root>/use_const_OR_ffttft' */  void issue_example_1214588_use_const_OR_ffttft(void)  {  ? static const boolean_T tmp[6] = { true, false, true, true, false, true };   ?
Workaround: Utilize a numeric type other than boolean to represent the array constant.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when copying blocks from a Simulink model using a data dictionary
Description: When copying blocks from one Simulink model to another, MATLAB will crash if the following conditions are true:  The model that the blocks are coming from is using a data dictionary that contains enumerated types.  There are no data dictionaries open in the Model Explorer or the MATLAB command window.
Workaround: Keep any data dictionary open while copying the blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Switch or Multiport Switch block in a multi-instance reusable subsystem can generate wrong code
Description: Your Simulink model can generate wrong code on building the model, if it meets all the following criteria: Your model has two or more identical atomic subsystems having a Switch or Multiport Switch block inside the subsystem. The control port of these blocks are driven by Constant blocks outside the subsystem. The values of the Constant block are such that they allow the Switch or Multiport Switch  block to select one input in one atomic subsystem and another input in another atomic subsystem.  On building this model, the generated code for the block does not have a conditional statement to handle the different inputs from the Constant block outside the subsystem. Instead, it is incorrectly optimized to take the input that is derived from the control port value of the instance of this subsystem that executes first. Hence, other instances may get the incorrect output from their Switch or Multiport Switch block.
Workaround: To work around this problem, clear the Inline Parameters check box in Model Configuration Parameters->Optimization->Signals and Parameters->Simulation and Code Generation and rebuild your model.Alternatively, you can add a Signal Conversion block after the Constant block.

Summary: Dashboard blocks lose settings and connection in Simulink Test harnesses
Description: This bug affects two related workflows involving Dashboard blocks inside a Simulink Test harness. If a test harness is created from a subsystem that contains a connected Dashboard block, the Dashboard block loses its connection when you close the test harness. When the test harness is closed, the Dashboard block shows the Double-click to connect  message indicating that the Dashboard block is not connected. If a Dashboard block is dropped inside a harness, any change in the block settings or connections are lost when the harness is closed and reopened. If you close the model, reopen the model, and open the test harness, then the Dashboard block shows the Double-click to connect  message indicating that it is not connected.

Summary: Invalid Simulink object handle error when generating tests for Stateflow charts or atomic subcharts containing model references
Description: Simulink Design Verifier displays the following error message when you generate tests for some Stateflow charts or atomic subcharts containing Simulink Functions with model reference blocks: Invalid Simulink object handle

Summary: Exporting a Simulink model that contains test harnesses to a previous Simulink version in MDL format silently discards harnesses and harness information
Description: If you export a Simulink model that contains one or more test harnesses to a previous version of Simulink in MDL format, the exported model generated by the export process does not contain the test harnesses or test harness information.  However, no warning is thrown to indicate that harnesses and harness information will not be saved. This occurs because test harnesses in Simulink Test are only supported in SLX format for R2015a (Simulink Test version 1.0). If you export a Simulink model that contains one or more test harnesses specifically to R2014b in SLX format, harnesses and harness information are not deleted even though it will not be possible to work with the harnesses in R2014b.

Summary: Code generation can fail for models with operator annotations enabled
Description: Generating code for a model with Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Comments  > Operator annotations  enabled can generate an error ?
Workaround: To avoid triggering this bug, disable operator annotations using one of these approaches:Clear the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Comments > Operator annotations parameter.At the MATLAB command line, enter:       set_param(bdroot, 'OperatorAnnotations', 'off');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: sort can produce incorrect results when sorting multiple columns of an array?
Description: sort  can produce incorrect results or crash when sorting multiple columns of a complex array. The bug does not occur with real input, when sorting along a dimension other than the first dimension, or when sorting column or row vectors. For the syntax [B,I] = sort(A) , when sorting multiple columns of a real or complex array that contains NaNs, the elements of the second output, I , might be incorrect. The elements of I  that refer to non-NaN s are correct. However, except for the first column, the elements of I  that refer to NaN s might be incorrect. The bug does not occur when sorting along a dimension other than the first dimension or when sorting column or row vectors.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for multiple calls to Rte_Read within a runnable for an?explicit read root inport branched to non-reusable subsystem
Description: The code generator produces?multiple calls?to an RTE read function (Rte_Read ) within a runnable if all of these conditions are true:? Model includes a root inport configured with AUTOSAR explicit read access. The root inport is branched;?it?connects to?at least?two reads within the same runnable boundary. One of the reads is within a non-reusable subsystem. That non-reusable subsystem is configured with a void-void interface   for code generation.  Under these conditions,?if the value returned by Rte_Read   changes?while the?runnable?executes, multiple reads can produce an?incorrect answer.
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block that directly connects to the root Inport?block (before branching) and select the block parameter?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization setting for the newly added block.

Summary: Incorrect analysis results caused by specified minimum and maximum values triggering fixed point optimizations
Description: Specified minimum and maximum values specified on blocks with fixed-point type outputs can incorrectly trigger certain fixed-point model optimizations for the destination blocks that consume those fixed-point outputs. The affected optimizations include removal of saturation semantics on integer overflow and in rare cases, choosing a smaller word length for the fixed point type. This bug can occur only when all the following conditions hold:A fixed point operation takes only fixed point inputs One of the fixed point inputs to the operation has a specified minimum maximum value on its actual source.

Summary: Cannot find devices when Data Acquisition Toolbox code is deployed
Description: Data Acquisition Toolbox code, such as daq.getDevices  or daq.getVendors , fail to find devices when deployed, even when the devices are connected and properly configured. If you're using DirectSound, apply the workarounds in EBR 1205811  as well as this EBR.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, you can do one of the following:Workaround 1Add the following line anywhere in your MATLAB code and recompile:%#function registerrealtimecataloglocationWorkaround 2If using mcc to compile your code, pass the-a registerrealtimecataloglocationoption to the mcc command.Workaround 3If using deploytool, add the registerrealtimecataloglocation mex function from the matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hwconnectinstaller directory to your deployment project as an additional required file.  matlabroot is your MATLAB installation directory, such as: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015a

Summary: MATLAB can crash when Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks do not check dynamic memory allocation success before first access
Description: Stateflow charts and MATLAB Function blocks use C dynamic memory allocation functions such as malloc and calloc to allocate space for instance variables for simulation. Simulink sometimes does not check the validity of the pointers returned by such functions before using them. If Simulink does not check the pointer validity, allocations can fail due to insufficient contiguous memory on the heap. This results in a crash. This occurs more commonly on 32-bit platforms. This bug affects only the simulation environment. This bug does not affect code generated by Simulink Coder or Embedded Coder.

Summary: "Element name mismatch" diagnostic does not work when set to error
Description: If you set the Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Connectivity > Element name mismatch  diagnopstic to error , then when a Bus Creator block passes a bus signal without bus object to an Inport block with a bus object specified, the diagnostic does not report the error even if the bus signal has different element names than the definition in the bus object.
Workaround: Specify the bus object at the Bus Creator block.

Summary: Unused function might be generated in export function-call subsystems or export function models
Description: An unused function can be generated for virtual subsystems containing more than one function-call subsystem whose outputs are connected to a Merge or Mux block. The functions are exported by right-clicking the virtual subsystem and choosing 'C/C++ Code ' > 'Export Functions '.  Starting in R2015a, an unused function can also be generated for referenced export function models under the same conditions.

Summary: Unable to run a verification or save changes to a project
Description: When Polyspace User Interface opens, the top right corner shows a message saying "Polyspace engine loading" and a progress bar. In certain cases, the engine loads to 99% and then stops, never reaching 100%.Because the engine is not fully loaded, the "Run" button and "Save" options are not active. Users are unable to run a verification or save changes to project.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in C:\Program Files\MATLAB or in /usr/local/MATLAB on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. polyspace is one of the principal sub-folders of <MATLAB>.Close the Polyspace User Interface (UI).Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate 'read & execute' permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Reopen the Polyspace UI.

Summary: During update diagram, Stateflow chart containing graphical function generates error
Description: A Stateflow chart containing a graphical function can generate the following error stack trace during update diagram "failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\export\include\cg_ir\type\type.hpp at line 431:  T::isa((const CG::Type*)aType)" The error is triggered when a chart has these conditions: 1) Stateflow chart is using MATLAB as the action language. 2) Chart has a graphical function containing a "For Loop" pattern. 3) The loop index variable is not defined by a Stateflow data object.
Workaround: Define the loop index variable with a chart level data object of "Local" scope.

Summary: Performance regression for models that use parameter structures
Description: You can use the fields of structures to set block parameter values in a model. For example, you can use the field myParams.myGain  in a Gain block, and the field myParams.myOffset  in a Constant block. In R2015a, models that use parameter structures simulate more slowly than in previous releases.

Summary: UI components inside uipanel objects disappear in GUIDE
Description: When viewing a UI layout in GUIDE, the components inside a uipanel  disappear when the Visible  property of the uipanel  object is set to 'off' . However, MATAB? displays the contents of the uipanel  display at run-time.

Summary: Parameter values in generated test cases might not match the dimension of the parameter object
Description: Test generation might give incorrect results if all of the following conditions are true:   You are using the parameter tuning workflow of Simulink Design Verifier  One of the tuned model parameters is described using a Simulink.Parameter object in the workspace  The tuned Simulink.Parameter object has a non-scalar dimension  Test generation will succeed, but the generated tests might have values whose dimensions do not match the Simulink.Parameter dimension. Creating a test harness and simulating the harness model will result in the error Size mismatch for MATLAB expression .

Summary: Error when exported function model has more than 50 root Inport blocks
Description: A model with a referenced export-function model causes an error on building when all  of the following conditions occur: The export-function model has a large number of root level Inport blocks (typically more than 50). The export-function model has more than one exported function. One or more of the Inport blocks with data outputs feed multiple exported functions.  The error is:  The number of columns specified did not match the actual number of columns in all of the rows.
Workaround: Use either of these approaches to fix the issue:Reduce the number of Inport blocks at the root level of the export-function model.Ensure that the root-level Inport blocks that output data signals within the export-function model only feed a single exported function.

Summary: False pass results when using HDL testbench
Description: The HDL testbench generated by HDL Coder can give a false pass test result because of the following issues:  When the value of IgnoreDataChecking  is greater than 0 clock cycles, the HDL testbench silently does not validate the results of the HDL simulation. The HDL testbench completion logic is considering the HDL testbench simulation to be complete one clock cycle before the final output of the design is generated. Therefore, the testbench value in the last cycle is not being validated.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when generating code for a reusable Function-Call Subsystem fed by a virtual bus
Description: MATLAB may crash when generating code for a model when all four of the following conditions are present: A reusable Function-Call Subsystem has a virtual bus input The virtual bus contains elements of different data types The subsystem's parent is an atomic subsystem The virtual bus originates outside the atomic (parent) subsystem
Workaround: Change the virtual bus to a nonvirtual bus by manually inserting a Signal Conversion block (either outside the parent subsystem or inside the parent subsystem) or by configuring the bus to nonvirtual at its source.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier harnesses created for some models with tunable parameters and model references do not behave as expected
Description: Design Verifier harnesses created for models that satisfy all  of the following conditions do not show the expected simulation behavior:  The model references another model. The model has parameter configuration enabled. The referenced model has at least one configured parameter.
Workaround: If Design Verifier analysis has not started: Load only the top model to be analysed by Design Verifier. Do not load any of the referenced models.For the top model, select the Analysis > Design Verifier > Options > Results > Save test harness as model parameter.Start Design Verifier analysis.         The harness model created after the analysis will show the correct behavior.If Design Verifier analysis is complete: Use the sldvruntest function to simulate the test cases or counter examples in the generated sldvData file.

Summary: Simulating a generated test-case might result in a Simulink error.
Description: A Simulink error is thrown when attempting to simulate a generated test case under the following conditions: Model contains Lookup Table blocks whose entries are specified using parameters. The Lookup Table parameters have constraints specified on them. The generated test case results in Lookup Table entries that are not monotonically increasing.  The following error is thrown when simulating the test case:  Parameter values of InputValues in 'ModelName/Look-Up Table' must be monotonically increasing before and after conversion to its run-time data type.
Workaround: Disable constraints for Lookup Table parameters.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Initial value from Simulink.Signal object does not appear in generated code for output of Variant Subsystem or Concatenate block.
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when all of the following conditions apply: A Simulink.Signal object with a non-zero initial value is attached to the output signal of a Variant Subsystem or a Concatenate block. The data type of the output signal of the Variant Subsystem or Concatenate block is not double . For the case of a Variant Subsystem, at least one the choices is a non-virtual (atomic or conditionally executed) subsystem. For the case of a Concatenate block, at least one of the input signals of the Concatenate block is driven by a non-virtual subsystem.  When the above conditions are met, the initialization code for the initial value of the Simulink.Signal object will not be generated for elements of the Concatenate block that are originating from such non-virtual subsystems.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block at the output port of the Variant Subsystem or Concatenate block and move the Simulink.Signal object from the output port of the Concatenate block to the output port of the Signal Conversion block.

Summary: Probabalistic roadmap path planner uses incorrect connection distance
Description: The Probabilistic Roadmap (robotics.PRM ) path planner uses a different connection distance value than what is specified in the ConnectionDistance  for the construction of the roadmap, when the supplied binary occupancy grid (robotics.BinaryOccupancyGrid ) has resolution other than 1. For example, the problem manifests itself in the following code segment : resolution = 10;  desiredConnectionDistance = 2;  map = robotics.BinaryOccupancyGrid(5, 5, resolution);  prm = robotics.PRM(map);  prm.ConnectionDistance = desiredConnectionDistance;  show(prm)  In the figure, you will see that distances between some of the connected nodes are larger than the desired connection distance of 2. The PRM object uses the connection distance of 20 instead.
Workaround: The above behavior is caused because ConnectionDistance is calculated as the product of ConnectionDistance and the binary occupancy grid's resolution. In the example above, a map resolution of 10 and desired connection distance of 2 resulted in an actual connection distance of 20. As a workaround to this issue, you can specify the ConnectionDistance to be the quotient of the originally desired connection distance and the map resolution. For example, the above code could be modified as follows:resolution = 10;desiredConnectionDistance = 2;map = robotics.BinaryOccupancyGrid(5, 5, resolution);prm = robotics.PRM(map);prm.ConnectionDistance = desiredConnectionDistance/resolution;show(prm)?

Summary: Build error or incorrect executable for parallel model rebuild
Description: A top model build in which referenced models are built in parallel can fail or build incorrectly if all  of the following conditions are true: You build the top model successfully, including parallel building of referenced models. After the build, you change a configuration option or parameter for the referenced models. You initiate another build of the top model.  Under these conditions, the build can fail with an error or the generated executable can behave incorrectly. Note: This issue is caused by an incorrectly generated makefile and is not an issue with the generated code itself. Generated code compiled outside of the Simulink Coder build process is not impacted.
Workaround: To work around this bug, delete the code generation folder between successive parallel model builds.To fix the bug, install the attached patch**.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Incorrect code generation for partitioned parameter used by nested For Each subsystem
Description: A model generates incorrect code if all of these conditions are true: The model contains a For Each subsystem. This subsystem partitions some mask parameter. The For Each subsystem contains another For Each subsystem. This inner subsystem contains blocks that refer to the mask parameter that the outer subsystem partitions. The inner subsystem is reusable in the generated code. For example, on the Code Generation  tab of the subsystem parameters dialog box, you set Function packaging  to Reusable function . The outer subsystem is set to generate non-reusable function .
Workaround: Open the subsystem parameters dialog box for the outer For Each subsystem. On the Code Generation tab, set Function packaging to Inline.

Summary: Assorted Model Slicer issues in dialog, generating slice models, and targeting slicing to a subsystem
Description: This bug report includes patches for the following Model Slicer bugs found in R2015a:  Sliced model generation can fail under any of the following conditions:Original model contains a referenced model and uses Configuration Reference Inactive elements of bus signals are grounded in the sliced model and error messages are thrown in Diagnostic Viewer due to name mismatch A root-level input port uses enumerated data type and is inactive The initial value of an inactive subsystem output of enumerated data type is used by a downstream block A disconnected line remains in the sliced model for input signals of an If block and a SwitchCase block A disconnected line remains in the sliced model when the second port of a Multiport Switch block is inactive and its upstream block is a subsystem Inactive lines between a Mux block and a root-level inport are retained in the sliced model   The sliced model produces false-positive warning messages about sorted execution order changes when the sliced model contains Data Store Memory blocks Some parts are unintentionally accessible in the Model Slice Manager dialog if the model fails to compile when Model Slicer is invoked Inactive input signals for a Merge block are highlighted when the simulation time window is applied with downstream signal propagation Issues when a slice subsystem is specified: Downstream highlighting throws an error about Struct contents reference from a non-struct array object  when the  slice subsystem contains another subsystem. Simulation time window cannot be applied if a slice subsystem is selected from a referenced model. Simulation time window cannot be applied if a slice subsystem contains a Data Store Read or Write block whose signal dimension is neither scalar nor 1xN.

Summary: Simulink may produce an invalid diagnostic when simulating a Simulink Function block in rapid accelerator mode
Description: If a Simulink Function block contains an Argument Inport block that is connected directly to a Scope block or to a Stateflow chart, Simulink may report a diagnostic such as: Unable to build a standalone executable to simulate the model 'MyModel' in rapid accelerator mode. The signal leaving the Argument Inport block 'My/Simulink Function/u' must not be testpointed or have a non-auto storage class.
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block between the Argument Input block and the Scope block or Stateflow chart.

Summary: Dashboard blocks inside custom library instances lose settings and connection
Description: This bug affects custom library workflows involving Dashboard blocks. When a custom library that contains Dashboard blocks is instantiated in a model, any change to the Dashboard block settings and connection is lost when the model is saved and closed. The next time the model is opened, the Dashboard block shows the Double-click to connect  message, indicating that the Dashboard block is not connected. Additionally, when reopened, the model shows unsaved changes exist even though no changes were made.

Summary: When you apply changes from the Simulink Function block dialog box, MATLAB can crash
Description: When you click Apply  or OK  on the dialog box of a Simulink Function block that is inside a Stateflow chart, MATLAB can crash.
Workaround: Before clicking on Apply or OK, navigate out of the parent Stateflow chart in all open editors.

Summary: AUTOSAR validation error for export-function model that reuses local Goto and From block tags at different model levels
Description: When an AUTOSAR export-function model reuses local Goto and From block tags at different model levels, one of two possible errors occurs. For example, an error occurs if a model includes Goto and From blocks that use tag MYTAG  at root /Subsystem1  and includes Goto and From blocks that use the same tag at root/Subsystem1/Subsystem1 . The errors that can occur are: Maximum recursion limit. For example, Maximum recursion limit of 500 reached. Use set(0,'RecursionLimit',N) to change the limit. Be aware that exceeding your available stack space can crash MATLAB and/or your computer.  Component: AUTOSAR Validation | Category: Simulink Mapping error   Inport connected to the From block needs to be connected to a function-call subsystem. For example,  Block 'i2_model2_nok/In1' may only be connected to a function-call subsystem

Summary: Incremental parallel build might fail after model changes that affect code generation
Description: A parallel build of a model might fail if the following conditions exist: Generated files from a previous parallel build persist in the build folder. You made changes to a model that is referenced by the top model from which you are generating code, and those changes affect the code interface of the model. For example, you added or removed a root port of the child model, which changes its generated model interface.  Under these conditions, the build process might fail to correctly reconcile the model changes with the build folder contents. The build fails with compilation or linking errors. ?

Summary: MATLAB can crash when you copy or paste a block that has a test harness whose name conflicts with an open Simulink model
Description: If you copy a block that has a test harness, and the test harness name conflicts with the name of an open Simulink model, MATLAB crashes. Also, if you paste a block with one or more test harnesses, and a new auto-generated test harness name conflicts with an open Simuink model, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: When you reopen the mask of a Signal Builder block that contains a large number of signals, the block generates an error.
Description: When you add 60 or more signals to the Signal Builder block, the block warns that the Window is too small . When you close and reopen the mask of that Signal Builder block, the block displays an error in the MATLAB Command Window.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Noncompilable code can be generated when models contain either an S-Function, Model block, or some built-in blocks
Description: Embedded Coder can generate noncompilable code for a model if all  of the following conditions are true: The model contains an S-Function, Model block, or some built-in blocks. The S-Function or Model block has an input from a Constant block. The Constant block has a mathematical expression written in its value field.  The built-in blocks include the Integrator Limited, Transport Delay, Variable Time Delay, Variable Transport Delay, Discrete State-Space, Discrete Zero-Pole, 1-D Lookup Table, 2-D Lookup Table, and n-D Lookup Table blocks.  Under these conditions, the generated code contains a dereference to a variable that is neither a pointer nor an array and the code cannot be compiled. This is likely to appear as a compiler error similar to:C2100: illegal indirection
Workaround: Use one of these approaches to work around this bug:Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Constant block and the problematic downstream block. In the Signal Conversion block, select the option Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.Create a Test Point on the signal connecting the two blocks: right-click the signal, select Signal Properties,and then check Test Point.?

Summary: Changing directories within the MATLAB? desktop appears slower than in previous releases.
Description: MATLAB? experiences a decrease in performance when displaying the content of certain network directories in Windows?.  This is typically experienced when the following conditions are true:  There is a virtual view pointing to folders (for example, Distributed File System (DFS)). There are folders within the virtual view that do not exist.
Workaround: To work around this issue, eliminate non-existent locations within the virtual view.

Summary: Polyspace for Simulink error during code generation analysis results review: No source code available for this item
Description: When you review analysis results for code generated with Embedded Coder, you can select certain links to highlight the related blocks in your model. If you have a coding rule violation and a back-to-model link on the same line of code, when you select the link, this error message can appear:  Error: No source code available for this item.  Afterward, you can no longer see the complete source code in the Polyspace user interface.
Workaround: Installation instructions for release R2015aLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation. By default this is in c:\Program Files\MATLAB or in /usr/local/MATLAB on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. polyspace is one of the principal sub-folders of <MATLAB>.Close the Polyspace User Interface (Polyspace UI).Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ˇ®read & executeˇŻ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Reopen the Polyspace UI.

Summary: Assertion when using variable-size arrays
Description: MATLAB?  can crash when generating C/C++ code from  MATLAB code that has the pattern  y = [y; u];  where  y  and u  are Nx1 matrices and u  is a variable-size array with all fixed-size dimensions. To see an example of this problem, generate code for the attached file. ?
Workaround: For a MATLAB statement having the formy = [y; u]; change y and u to 1xN or make u a fixed-size variable.

Summary: In Simcape language, literal values in 'deg' are handled incorrectly in the equation section
Description: In the equation section of a component file, if you specify a literal value in 'deg' , it is handled incorrectly. For example, { 180 'deg' } is actually treated as { 180 'rad' }. Angular velocity and other compound units involving angles are handled correctly.
Workaround: When using literal angular values, use radians.

Summary: Simulink.NumericType parameters are not listed in the Parameter Table in Simulink Design Verifier options
Description: In the Configuration Parameters > Design Verifier > Parameters  pane, the Find in Model  operation of the Parameter Table  does not list model parameters that are of type Simulink.NumericType . This prevents the application of constraints to these parameters for achieving full coverage.
Workaround: Constraints for Simulink.NumericType parameters can be specified using a Parameter Configuration File.

Summary: Execution timeline graphic from profile_xpc shows wrong core number for preempted task
Description: The profile_xpc  function generates a graphic that shows the sequence of task activation and preemption over the execution timeline. The function generates the graphic using data recorded during code execution on the target hardware. For multicore hardware, the data includes the number of the core on which each task runs on. In some cases, the graphic shows the wrong core number for a task that has resumed following preemption.

Summary: Copy and paste of both a subchart and supertransition causes a crash
Description: MATLAB can crash when you copy and paste a selection that includes both a Stateflow subchart and a supertransition.
Workaround: First copy and paste just the subchart(s), then the copy and paste the supertransition(s).

Summary: Setting the units of UI objects inside GUIDE to normalized can corrupt the UI file
Description: In GUIDE, changing the units of UI objects to normalized units can cause the UI file to become corrupt and lose data. The following must be true for this corruption to occur: The UI has container objects (such as a uipanel or uibuttongroup object) in a hierarchical sequence. For example, a uipanel is contained within another uipanel. You changed the units of a UI object to normalized units using one of the following techniques: Setting the Resize Behavior  of the UI to Proportional  in the GUI Options dialog. Setting the Units  property of a UI object to 'normalized' .   Using normalized units can have the following effects on the UI when displayed through GUIDE: The UI objects disappear from the UI. The position and layout information of UI objects change from their original configuration.  ?
Workaround: Recovering corrupted UI files:Although the UI objects appear to be missing, they still exist in GUIDE. You can recover some information in your UI file by first making the UI objects reappear, and then changing their units to something other than 'normalized'.To make the UI objects reappear, move the parent container slightly. For example, if the missing object is inside a uipanel, then select the uipanel and move it slightly using one of arrow keys on your keyboard.When the missing object becomes visible again, change its Units property to any value other than 'normalized' (for example, 'characters').Avoid setting the Units property back to 'normalized'. Otherwise the problem will reoccur when you save, close and reopen the file in GUIDE.You might need to adjust the layout of your UI after you restore the UI objects.Enable UI resizing after recovering corrupted UI files:To make your UI resize proportionally, open your program file, and add the following code to the function that has _OpeningFcn in the name. hfig = hObject;% Set the figure visible in order to make sure the rendering finisheshfig.Visible = 'on';drawnow;% Enable resize of the figurehfig.Resize = 'on';% Change Units of each object to normalizedhAllObjects = findobj(hfig);for i = 1:numel(hAllObjects)    hAllObjects(i).Units = 'normalized';endThe attached GUIDE files (exampleWorkaround.m and exampleWorkaround.fig) show how to implement the code above. In this case, the code is added to the function, exampleWorkaround_OpeningFcn in exampleWorkaround.m.For more assistance, contact Technical Support by visiting the following page: https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html

Summary: Model reference hierarchy containing a non-inlined TLC S-Function that can be inlined inside a reusable subsystem might error out during accelerator mode simulation
Description: A model that is inside a model reference hierarchy may error out in accelerator mode if the following conditions exist: The model contains a subsystem with Function packaging  set to Reusable function . The reusable subystem contains a TLC S-function block that is configured to be inlined in the generated code with SS_OPTION_USE_TLC_WITH_ACCELERATOR .  If the model errors out, you will get the following error message: Error: File: matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/globalsysvar.tlc Line: 121 Column: 9 Undefined identifier tsysRTM

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generating code from a model configured for concurrent execution might produce a crash
Description: Generating code from a model might produce a crash when all of these conditions are true:  The model is configured for concurrent execution The target architecture contains hardware nodes A model reference block with unconnected input ports is mapped to a hardware node

Description: If the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Custom Code > Include directories  parameter uses a relative path token to the parent folder (e.g., "../ "), the packNGo  command does not include the source files from that folder.

Description: If you have a subchart inside a subchart with a supertransition, and you delete the inner subchart and then undo the action, MATLAB might crash.

Description: The following parameters in the 3DOF and 6DOF blocks are marked as tunable: Initial position  Initial velocity  Initial orientation  Initial incidence  Initial rotation rates  Initial mass  Inertia  Empty inertia matrix  Full Inertia matrix  Empty mass  Full mass   However, these parameters are not functionally tunable. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when an unassigned structure array is passed to the?size function
Description: Passing?an?unassigned?structure array to the size  function in your MATLAB code causes?the generated code?to have?undefined behavior. This can cause the generated code for the size  function to return a random?value and, as a result,?behave differently than the?MATLAB code. MATLAB would error. For example, the MATLAB function size2(u) defined?below?has undefined behavior in generated code if u >= 1 . function y = size2(u)if u < 1    t = struct('a',1,'b',2);    x = repmat(t,1,3);endy = size(x,1)  % Result of size(x,1) is undefined if u >= 1. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: Before passing a structure array to size in your MATLAB code, define it along all possible execution paths.

Summary: If blocks with multiple Elseif conditions cause errors
Description: If you run Simulink Design Verifier on a model that has If blocks with multiple Elseif expressions, you might encounter the following error: Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: < model > is incompatible with Simulink Design Verifier.

Summary: Deleting a Subsystem block in an open Simulink diagram can crash MATLAB
Description: When you delete a Subsystem block that has Simscape ports from a Simulink diagram (either in a model or library) that is open in a Simulink Editor window, there is a small chance that MATLAB?will crash.
Workaround: To delete that Subsystem block, do not open the system. Instead, load the system and use the command-line API to delete the block. For example:?load_system('sf_car')delete_block('sf_car/transmission')?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Buffer passed into LibMdlStartCustomCode does not appear in the generated code
Description: When the LibMdlStartCustomCode  TLC function is called, the buffer created by that function is ignored.
Workaround: Before generating the code, run the attached .p file .This .p  file disable a feature present in R2014b that changes how initialization code is processed.

Summary: Code generation?can fail to detect?the use of an unassigned?variable
Description: In MATLAB, using a variable that was not assigned before is an error.?This is also?true for code generation?from MATLAB. In certain cases, the?code?generator fails to detect?the use of a variable that is not assigned. In such cases, the code generator does not produce?a compilation error and proceeds with?code generation.? One specific?situation that is affected by this bug occurs when all of the following conditions?are true:? A?variable is assigned in one branch of a?if  or?switch  statement but not assigned in the other branch.  The variable is used only?inside size()  or numel() .  The size of the variable is fixed.   In this case, the?code generator takes the value of the variable?from the assignment in one branch, and uses it in?the other branch as well. For?example, consider this MATLAB function:? function out = foo(n)if n > 0    x = ones(3,4);elseendout = size(x,1);end? The intended behavior for code generation is to error ("Variable x is not assigned on some execution paths "), but instead the following C code is produced: out = 3;?  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks?in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: To?determine if your Simulink model is affected by this issue, open the model in R2015b release or newer and perform update diagram (ctrl+D). If you do not receive a compilation error about variable used but not assigned, then your model is not affected.To?determine if your MATLAB source code (used with MATLAB Coder) is affected, use MATLAB Coder from R2015b release or newer. If code generation does not issue an error about variable used but not assigned, then your source code is not affected.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer between simulation and generated code for Interpolation using Prelookup block with?Saturate on integer overflow?check box selected and?Intermediate results Data Type?set to fixed-point data type
Description: Incorrect?code might be generated from the Interpolation using Prelookup block?when these?conditions?are all true: The Saturate on integer overflow  check box is selected  The?Interpolation method ?is set to?Linear  Table Data Type ,?Intermediate results Data Type ,?and?Output Data Type ?are fixed-point data types Table Data Type ?and?Output Data Type ?have the same slope adjustment factor and bias Table Data Type ?does not overflow if cast to?Intermediate results Data type ?or the?Output Data Type   Intermediate results Data type ?may overflow if cast to?Output Data Type  The Integer Rounding mode  is set to either:Ceiling , Convergent , Round , or Nearest  when?the Table data  Value is close to the maximum value of the data type Floor , Simplest , or?Zero  when the Table data  Value is close to the minimum value of the data type    If these conditions are all present,?a wrapping effect may occur instead of a saturation effect in the output of the Interpolation using Prelookup block.
Workaround: Set the??Output data type?parameter of the Interpolation Using Prelookup block to?the same data type as the?Intermediate results data typeAdd and connect a?Data Type Conversion block to the output of the Interpolation Using Prelookup blockSelect the?Saturate on integer overflow?check box of the Data Type Conversion blockSet the?Output data type?parameter of the?Data Type Conversion block to the desired data type

Description: Navigating into and out of subsystems in large Simulink models can be slow.
